INCLUSION OF JHODIA COMMUNITY IN THE STs LIST
1401. SHRI SAPTAGIRI SANKAR ULAKA:
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any proposal to include Jhodia community in the
list of Scheduled Tribes (STs), if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons
therefor;
(b) the process by which Jhodia community would be included in the said list;
(c) whether the recommendation of the State Government of Odisha for inclusion
of certain communities in the list of STs is pending with the Union Government;
and
(d) if so, the details thereof and the reasons for the pendency?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGADAS UIKEY):
(a) to (d): The proposal from State Government of Odisha has been received for
inclusion of Jhodia community in Scheduled Tribes list of Odisha.
The Government of India on 15.6.1999 (further amended on 25.6.2002 and
14.9.2022) has laid down the modalities for deciding the claims for inclusion in,
exclusion from and other modifications in Orders specifying Scheduled Castes and
Scheduled Tribes lists. As per the modalities, only those proposals which have
been recommended and justified by the concerned State Government / UT
1 3.02.2025 216
Administration and concurred with by Registrar General of India (RGI) and National
Commission for Scheduled Tribes (NCST) are to be considered and legislation
amended. All action on the proposals is taken as per these approved modalities.
The proposals for inclusion in the list of Scheduled Tribes of a State or Union
Territory follow certain processes as per the modalities. This is a continuous
process. Proposals received from the State Government should be accompanied
by an ethnographic report. The proposals are examined by the Office of the RGI
and then by the NCST. In case the proposal is not recommended by the RGI, the
State Governments are communicated the points raised by the RGI, so that
additional information, if any, maybe furnished by the State Government. Many
such proposals may therefore remain under examination at different levels.
SAGARMALA HIGHWAY PROJECT IN KERALA
1402. SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to
state:
(a) the current status of the Sagarmala Highway project in Kerala, including the
details of its planning, approvals and implementation progress;
(b) the specific stretches of the Sagarmala Highway proposed or under construction
in Kerala, and the expected timelines for their completion;
1 3.02.2025 217
(c) whether any delays have been encountered in the project due to land
acquisition, environmental clearances, or other factors, if so, the steps taken
thereof to resolve them;
(d) the total budget allocated for the implementation of the Sagarmala Highway
project in Kerala and the amount utilized so far;
(e) the manner in which the Sagarmala Highway project is expected to benefit
Kerala's economy, with a particular focus on trade, tourism and coastal
development;
(f) the measures being taken to ensure minimal environmental impact and
compliance with regulations; and
(g) the details of collaboration between the Union and State Government for the
successful implementation of the Sagarmala Highway project in Kerala?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a) to (c) There is no scheme / programme under the name “Sagarmala Highway”.
However, Sagarmala programme is the flagship programme of the Government in
Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways to promote port-led development in the
country through harnessing India’s 7,500 km long coastline, 14,500 km of
potentially navigable waterways and strategic location on key international
maritime trade routes.
1 3.02.2025 218
Government in Ministry of Road Transport and Highways is currently pursuing 3
Port Connectivity Projects in the State of Kerala as given in the enclosed
Statement which are at a Detailed Project Report (DPR) stage. Based on the
outcome of the DPR, further action will be taken.
(d) to (g) Does not arise.
STATEMENT
Details of 03 Port Connectivity projects in Kerala.
Port Type of Port Project Name Status
1 Cochin Major Four laning of NH 966 B connecting W/Island to Kundanoor. DPR in
progress
2 Azhikkal Non-Major Azhikkal Port - Proposed NH – Bypass and widening of 2 km. DPR in
progress
3 Alappuzha Non-Major Providing ramps to the 6 lane elevated highway being developed DPR in
from the Alappuzha beach (Earlier - Road from SH-Alappuzha progress
bypass intersection)
OUTREACH AND EFFECTIVENESS OF JJM IN TIRUPATI DISTRICT
1403.SHRI MADDILA GURUMOORTHY:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the number of SC/ST/OBC households provided with tap water connections
under the Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) in Tirupati district;
1 3.02.2025 219
(b) the percentage of SC/ST-dominated habitations fully covered under the JJM in
the saiddistrict and the status of coverage across the State;
(c) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to ensure community
participation,particularly of women, in water management committees as part
of the said mission and theimpact of these steps in Tirupati district; and
(d) the growth trends of average per capita, per day (pc/pd) tap water availability
during thelast five years, State-wise, with specific reference to Tirupati district,
in light of the national target of 55 litres per capita per day (pc/pd)?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V. SOMANNA):
(a) to (d): Government of India is committed to make provision for safe and potable
tap water supply in adequate quantity i.e. 55 LPCD, of prescribed quality and on a
regular and long-term basis to all rural households in the country. Towards this end,
since August 2019, Government of India is implementing Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM)
in partnership with States to make provision of tap water supply to every rural
household. Water being a state subject, the responsibility of planning, approval,
implementation, operation, and maintenance of drinking water supply schemes/
works, including those under the Jal Jeevan Mission, lies with State/UT
Governments. The Government of India supports the States by providing technical
and financial assistance.
1 3.02.2025 220
At the time of launch of JJM, in Andhra Pradesh, 30.74 Lakhs rural households
(32.18%) of the State had tap water connections. Since then, about 39.72 Lakhs
households in rural areas have been provided with tap water connections and as
on 09.02.2025, out of total 95.53 Lakhs rural households in the State, about 70.46
Lakhs (73.76%) households are having tap water connections including 8.66 lakhs
SC and 3.78 lakhs ST household, respectively.
Similarly, at launch of JJM, around 2.40 Lakhs rural households (57.58%) in
Tirupati district of Andhra Pradesh had tap water connections. Since then, about
1.76 Lakhs households in rural areas have been provided with tap water
connections. As on 09.02.2025, out of total 4.17 Lakhs rural households in the
district, around4.16 Lakhs (99.82%) households are having tap water connections
including 1.19 LakhsSC and 0.26 Lakhs ST households, respectively. Data of OBC
is not maintained by this Department.
As per information provided by State Government, as on 09.02.2025, there are total
of 4,018 habitations in the Tirupati district, of which 487 habitations are marked as
‘ST-concentrated’ and 1,331 habitations are marked as ‘SC-concentrated’. Out of
these, 485 ‘ST- concentrated’ and 1,328 ‘SC-concentrated’ habitations have been
saturated as on 09.02.2025.
To encourage the community participation, the operational guideline for the
implementation of JJM provide for constitution of a Village Water and Sanitation
Committee (VWSC)/ Paani Samiti/ User Group, etc. to carry out the responsibilities
of water supply management in the village. This Committee typically consists of 10-
1 3.02.2025 221
15 members including up to 25% elected panchayat members, 50% women, and
the members from weaker section (SC/ ST) in proportion to their population out of
the remaining 25%.
In Tirupati district, there are 908 villages out of which VWSCs have been formed in
901 villages. This has resulted in increased participation of women in water
management at village level.
The State-wise details of tap water connections provided under JJM during last 5
years is given in the enclosed Statement.
1 3.02.2025 222

1 3.02.2025 234
ACHIEVEMENTS OF SCM
1405. SHRI MANOJ TIWARI:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the most significant milestones achieved under the Smart
Cities Mission (SCM) since its inception;
(b) the manner in which the said mission has improved urban infrastructure
such as roads, water supply and waste management in the selected cities;
and
(c) whether the SCM has successfully completed and if so the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (c) Smart Cities Mission (SCM) was launched on 25.06.2015 with the
aim to promote cities by providing core infrastructure and give a decent
quality of life to its citizens through application of ‘Smart’ Solutions. The
implementation of SCM at the City level has been done by a Special Purpose
Vehicle (SPV). The mission followed an area-based development approach
through retrofitting, redevelopment and greenfield development in 100 cities.
This approach accounted for other projects in the selected area, from
multiple funding sources like convergence with other Central and State
Government schemes, Public Private Partnership etc.
1 3.02.2025 235
100 cities have undertaken a total of 8,058 projects amounting to ₹1,64,368
crore, in the mission. As on 20.01.2025, 7,480 projects (i.e. 93% of total
projects) amounting to ₹1,50,017 crore have been completed, as per the
data provided by 100 Smart Cities. Details of significant milestones achieved
under various core infrastructure sectors of SCM is given in the enclosed
Statement.
Under SCM, the Central Government has a total outlay of ₹48,000 crore for
the 100 cities. As on 20.01.2025, the cities have been able to claim central
financial assistance of ₹47,425 crore (i.e. 98.8% of the total Central share
allocation) as per the utilization of funds provided by them. The States/Union
Territories are reported to have utilized ₹45,506 crore (i.e. 96% of the total
Central share released).

REGISTRATIONS UNDER PM VISHWAKARMA SCHEME
1407. SHRI NAVEEN JINDAL:
Will the Minister of MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM ENTERPRISES be pleased
to state:
(a) the total number of Vishwakarma registered under PM Vishwakarma
Scheme so far;
(b) the details of the trades in which skill upgradation training is provided to help
artisans to hone their skills; and
(c) the steps taken by the Government to provide the intended beneficiaries an
easy access to collateral free credit and reduce the cost of credit by providing
interest subvention?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND
MEDIUM ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT (SUSHRI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a): The PM Vishwakarma Scheme was launched on 17.09.2023 to provide end-
to-end support to artisans and craftspeople of 18 trades who work with their
1 3.02.2025 241
hands and tools. As of 10.02.2025, 27.25 lakh beneficiaries have been
registered under the Scheme.
(b): The Scheme covers artisans and craftspeople engaged in 18 trades, in
which skill upgradation training is provided to help the Artisans hone their skills.
These trades include: (i) Carpenter (Suthar/Badhai); (ii) Boat Maker; (iii)
Armourer; (iv) Metalsmith/Metal Caster; (v) Hammer and Tool Kit Maker; (vi)
Locksmith; (vii) Goldsmith (Sonar); (viii) Potter (Kumhaar); (ix) Sculptor
(Moortikar, stone carver), Stone breaker; (x) Cobbler (Charmakar)/
Shoesmith/Footwear artisan; (xi) Mason (Rajmistri); (xii) Basket/Mat/Broom
Maker/Coir Weaver; (xiii) Doll and Toy Maker (Traditional); (xiv) Barber (Naai);
(xv) Garland maker (Malakaar); (xvi) Washerman (Dhobi); (xvii) Tailor (Darzi);
and (xviii) Fishing Net Maker.
(c): The credit component, implemented by Department of Financial Services
(DFS), focuses on providing affordable and easy access collateral-free
concessional credit for enterprise development. Under the Scheme, Loans are
provided to the beneficiaries in two tranches:
 Tranche 1: Loan up to Rs.1 lakh with a repayment period of 18 months.
 Tranche2: Loan up to Rs.2 lakh with a repayment period of 30 months.
The rate of interest charged to beneficiaries is only 5% with an interest
subvention of up to 8% is provided upfront by the Government to the banks.
Loan coverage is secured under the Credit Guarantee Fund Trust for Micro and
Small Enterprises on a portfolio basis. During basic skill training period,
beneficiaries are provided with half-day financial literacy sessions specifically
1 3.02.2025 242
designed to familiarize them with the loan features under the Scheme. The
Scheme is widely accessible through Public Sector Banks, Regional Rural
Banks, Cooperative Banks, and Private Sector Banks, ensuring outreach to
urban, rural, and remote areas across the country.
PRESERVATION AND PROMOTION OF TRIBAL CULTURAL HERITAGE
1408 : SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI:
SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
SHRIMATI D.K. ARUNA:
SHRI EATALA RAJENDER
Will the Minister of TRIBAL AFFAIRS be pleased to state
(a) the details of initiatives taken/being taken by the Government to promote
tribal culture and heritage during the last three years;
(b) the details of total number of conferences, cultural programs and exhibitions
organized along with their geographical distribution in the country, State-wise,
particularly in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh,
Chhattisgarh and Bihar;
(c) whether any conference has been organised by the Government alongwith
other organisations including NGOs, to document the linguistic resources of the
tribes, especially the tribal languages spoken in various States and if so, the
details and outcome thereof;
(d) the budgetary allocation for promoting tribal culture for the current fiscal year
and the funds utilized thereon so far; and
1 3.02.2025 243
(e) the details of the impact of these initiatives on preserving and promoting tribal
heritage, especially in tribal dominated areas during the last ten years?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TRIBAL AFFAIRS (SHRI
DURGADAS UIKEY):
(a) Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India, under the Centrally Sponsored
scheme ‘Support to Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs)’ provides financial support
to 29 Tribal Research Institutes (TRIs) in States/Union Territories (UTs) on the
basis of the Annual Action Plan submitted by the States/UTs subject to the
approval of the Apex Committee chaired by the Secretary, Ministry of Tribal
Affairs. Under the Scheme, proposals relating to infrastructural needs, research
and documentation activities and training and capacity building programmes,
organization of tribal festivals, yatras for promotion of unique cultural heritage
and tourism and organisation of exchange visits by tribals are organised so that
their cultural practices, languages and ritual are preserved and disseminated.
Ministry has sanctioned various projects to the Tribal Research Institutes of the
States/UTs towards activities for the socio-cultural, educational, and economic
development of the tribal communities. TRIs are primarily institutions under the
State Government/UT Administration. The activities undertaken by TRIs to
benefit the tribal communities are mentioned as under:
(i) To acknowledge the heroic and patriotic deeds of tribal people and to
exhibit rich tribal cultural heritage of the region, Ministry has sanctioned 11
Tribal Freedom Fighters Museums.
1 3.02.2025 244
(ii) Ministry has developed searchable digital repository where all
research papers, books, reports and documents, folks songs, photos/videos are
uploaded. The repository currently has more than 10,000 photographs, videos
and publications which are mostly done by Tribal Research Institutes.
The repositories can be visited at https://repository.tribal.gov.in/ (Tribal
Digital Document Repository) and https://tribal.nic.in/repository/ (Tribal
Repository).
(iii) State level festivals like Hornbill festival of Nagaland, Medaram Jatra
of Telangana are funded through TRI scheme. State Tribal festival, fair
and cultural programmes are funded by the Ministry of Tribal Affairs (MoTA)
to showcase glimpses of rich cultural heritage of tribal people across the
country through unique forms of folk dances, songs, cuisine, exhibition
and demonstration of traditional skill in painting, art and craft, medicinal
practices etc.
(iv) TRIFED organizes Tribal Artisan Melas (TAMs) to identify new artisans and
new products at the sourcing level in States/Districts/Villages for expanding the
tribal producers base.
(v) The Ethnographic Museum of the states preserve and showcase
rare artefacts related to life and culture of various tribes.
(vi) The Ministry also provide financial assistance under the scheme “Tribal
Research, Information, Education, Communication and Events (TRI-ECE)”, to
reputed research institutes/ organizations/ universities to undertake various
1 3.02.2025 245
research studies/publication of books/ documentation including audio visual
documentaries to fill the gap of research studies on tribal issues and Promotion
of rich tribal cultural, traditions and customs as well as capacity building of tribal
persons/ institutions associated with tribal affairs, dissemination of information
and creation of awareness.
(b) Under the scheme of Support to TRIs, Ministry has sanctioned various
projects/ activities wherein 100% funding is provided by the Central Government
on the proposal submitted by States. The details of total number of Conferences,
Cultural Programme and exhibitions sanctioned under the Support to Tribal
Research Institutes scheme may be viewed at Ministry's website
https://tribal.gov.in/Display_Apex_Minutes.aspx .
(c) Ministry of Tribal Affairs extends support to State Governments/UTs Tribal
Research Institutes under the Centrally Sponsored scheme ‘Support to Tribal
Research Institute(s)’ for development of bilingual Primers for preservation of
tribal languages and enhancement of learning achievements level amongst the
Scheduled Tribes students. States/UTs TRIs are conducting documentation and
preservation of endangered tribal languages through preparation of primers,
dictionaries and organization of cultural programmes. Further, under the scheme
"Tribal Research, Information, Education, Communication and Events (TRI-
ECE)", Ministry of Tribal Affairs provides financial assistance to reputed
Institutions for carrying out research study
programmes including documentation of tribal languages. In this regard,
1 3.02.2025 246
Ministry had sanctioned projects to Bhasha Research and Publication Centre
during 2018-19 and 2019-20 which inter-alia covered activity of identification and
documentation of threatened/dying languages. The organization had carried out
documentation of Korku, Nihali, Kolami, Vadi, Halpati, Dungra Bhili, Dhavadi,
Dhatti, Thali, Nahal and Seheriya languages.
(d) The budgetary allocation for promoting tribal culture for the current fiscal
year and the funds sanctioned may be viewed at Ministry's website
https://tribal.gov.in/Display_Apex_Minutes.aspx .
(e) The initiatives preserve the heritage, customs, culture and traditions of the
tribal communities for the future generations. Further, through these projects and
activities, tribal communities participate in conferences, seminars, workshops,
and exchange programs to abreast themselves about the various welfare and
development schemes/activities of the Government/Organization facilitating
socio-economic improvement/betterment in the life of the tribal communities.
Teachers from Government Ashram Schools along with language
experts from respective communities are associated in developing the
dictionaries and primers in tribal dialects and language, which not only preserves
and conserve the tribal languages but also help tribal students in their education
by facilitating basic learning in their own language/dialect from class I to III and
help in smooth transition when they go to higher classes. More than 200 primers
and dictionaries have been developed in different tribal languages by Tribal
1 3.02.2025 247
Research Institute(s) to support and encourage multilingual education among
tribal communities.
Through the State Tribal Festivals, fair and cultural programmes, Tribal
Artisan Melas and Aadi Mahotsav etc. new artisans and products have been
identified and promoted enabling commercial recognition and at the same time
generating income to the tribals, enhancing their livelihoods in
States/District/Village level. Organising state tribal festivals, fairs, and cultural
programs to showcase glimpses of the rich cultural heritage of tribal people
across the country through unique forms of folk dances, songs, cuisine,
exhibitions, and demonstration of traditional skills in painting, art, and craft,
medicinal practices, etc. All these activities have not only contributed to the
preservation, protection and promotion of tribal cultural heritage but also provide
unity and fraternity among the tribal communities.
िवमान संपक(cid:170) सेवा का िव(cid:214) तार

सहका(cid:229)रता म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा नागर िवमानन म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी मरु लीधर
मोहोल):
(क): देश म(cid:164) हवाई या(cid:253)ा को जनसाधारण के िलए िकफायती बनाने, और असेिवत एवं अ(cid:208)पसेिवत
हवाईअड्ड(cid:332) से (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय हवाई संपक(cid:170) बढ़ाने के िलए, सरकार ने अ(cid:179)टूबर, 2016 म(cid:164) (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय संपक(cid:170) योजना
(आरसीएस) - उड़ान (उड़े दशे का आम नाग(cid:229)रक) श(cid:322)ु क(cid:236) है। इस योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य उड़ान बोली
(cid:255)ि(cid:248)या के दौर(cid:332) के मा(cid:197)यम से िचि(cid:313)त मौजूदा हवाई प(cid:229)(cid:280)य(cid:332) को 'असेिवत और अ(cid:208)पसेिवत हवाई

LEADING PACKAGED FOOD COMPANIES
1410. SHRI KRIPANATH MALLAH:
SHRI ANUP SANJAY DHOTRE:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to
state:
(a) whether Government has made efforts to engage with leading packaged
food companies to address their concerns surrounding ease of doing
business, GST, imports and other trade-related matters and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) the details of the number of Companies participated in the discussions;
and
1 3.02.2025 250
(c) the details of the measures undertaken by Government to boost food
processing industry and increase the growth of exports, income of farmers
and employment opportunities?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) and (b) The Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has been actively
engaging with stakeholders across the food and allied sectors, including large,
medium and small enterprises in the packaged food segment to identify and
address challenges hindering the sector's growth. These deliberations aim to
unlock the sector's full potential and enhance the ease of doing business.
Stakeholder consultation meetings were held on 6th and 7th November, 2024 with
industry representatives and associations. The list of participants in the said
stakeholder consultations is given in the enclosed Statement.
(c) The Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) through implementation
of various initiatives and schemes viz. Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana
(PMKSY), Prime Minister Formalization of Micro Food Processing Enterprises
(PMFME) and Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing
Industries (PLISFPI) aims for the overall development of food processing sector.
Under various component of the aforementioned schemes, the MoFPI provides
financial assistance for creation of modern infrastructure with efficient supply
chain management from farm gate to retail outlet. The infrastructure created and
assistance provided aims at providing better returns to farmers, creation of
1 3.02.2025 251
employment opportunities, reducing wastage, increasing the processing level
and enhancing the export of the processed foods.

MALFUNCTIONING OF AIRCRAFTS
1411. SHRI K. RADHAKRISHNAN
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has noticed the cases of malfunctioning of
aircrafts of private airlines during the last two years resulting difficulties to
air passengers, particularly elderly and children and if so, the details
thereof, airline-wise; and
1 3.02.2025 252
(b) the action taken by the Government against such airlines?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) : During operations, an aircraft may experience technical snags due to
malfunctioning of components/ equipment fitted on the aircraft which require
rectification action by the airlines for continued safe, efficient and reliable air
transport service. These technical snags are reported by the flight crew on
receiving and aural/ visual warning in the cockpit or an indication of an
inoperative / faulty system or while experiencing difficulty in handling/
operating the aircraft. The snag is thereafter rectified as per the procedure in
the AMM and may involve replacement of components, testing, servicing etc.
The details of cases of malfunctioning of aircrafts of private airlines during
the last two years is given in the enclosed Statement.
(b) : DGCA has laid down regulations under Civil Aviation Requirement (CAR)
which requires that the aircraft is maintained in accordance with the
manufacturer and DGCA guidelines and all snags reported on the aircraft are
rectified before the aircraft is released for flight. Further, CAR 145 lays down
the requirements for the approval of maintenance organization which
mandates the organization to have required manpower, equipment and
literature commensurate to the type and fleet to be maintained. Under the
system, airlines are required to ensure that the aircraft are maintained in a
1 3.02.2025 253
continuous state of airworthiness and all defects are rectified. DGCA has a laid
down mechanism of conducting surveillance, spot checks, night
surveillance etc. of the airlines/ organizations and personnel to ensure all
regulatory requirements are complied with to ensure safety of passenger and
aircraft. The observations/ findings made during surveillance, spot checks and
night surveillance are provided to the airline for taking corrective action. The
action taken to correct the observation are reviewed to enforce corrective and
preventive action. In case of violations, DGCA takes enforcement action in
accordance with laid down procedures which may consist of warning,
suspension, cancellation including imposition of financial penalty to the
personnel involved/ airline.
STATEMENT

HIKE IN AIRFARE
1413. SHRI YUSUF PATHAN:
SUSHRI SAYANI GHOSH:
SHRI Y. S. AVINASH REDDY:
ADV. PRIYA SAROJ:
DR. M.K. VISHNU PRASAD:
THIRU DAYANIDHI MARAN:
SHRI UMESHBHAI BABUBHAI PATEL:
SHRI NAVASKANI K.:
ADV. ADOOR PRAKASH:
1 3.02.2025 256
SHRI N. K. PREMACHANDRAN:
SHRI RAJEEV RAI:
SHRI JANARDAN SINGH SIGRIWAL:
SHRI CHARANJIT SINGH CHANNI :
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government is aware of the recent surge in airfares during
festive seasons and peak travel periods across the country, including the recent
example of Kumbh, Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh which leads to make air travel
unaffordable for the common people and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the specific measures being taken by the Government to regulate and control
such price hikes, especially for routes connecting Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities;
(c) whether the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has issued any
guidelines to rationalize airfares during the said periods and if so, the details
thereof;
(d) whether the Government is considering capping airfares during emergencies,
natural disasters and medical emergency to ensure affordability for the
passengers and if so, the details thereof;
(e) whether the Government proposes to amend the Airports Economic
Regulatory Authority (AERA) Act to enhance oversight on fare increases and
protect consumer interests and if so, the details thereof along with the steps
taken in this regard;
(f) whether the Government proposes to fix the maximum ticket charge for a
particular destination and if so, the details thereof;
1 3.02.2025 257
(g) the amount paid to airlines under Viability Gap Funding to compensate for
low fares, year and airline-wise; and
(h) the measures taken by the Government to ensure transparency in airfare
pricing and make it mandatory for airlines to disclose fare components clearly?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (d), (f) and (h): Regular consultations are held with airlines and airlines are
sensitized to exercise moderation/ self- regulation while fixing the airfares and
to keep passengers' interest in mind. As the festival's peak days approach,
especially during the forthcoming Maha Kumbh's and other important Shahi
Snans, airlines have taken further steps to enhance capacity to accommodate
in order to ease pressure on airfare.;
The airfares to Prayagraj for the Maha Kumbh 2025 saw a decline after govt
intervention.
The airlines have committed to ensure that airfares do not surge during events
such as natural disasters, calamities, pandemic, Mahakumbh etc.;
Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has setup a Tariff Monitoring Unit
(TMU) that monitors airfares on select domestic sectors on random basis by
using airlines' websites on monthly basis to ensure that the airlines do not charge
airfares outside the range declared by them.;
1 3.02.2025 258
Rule 135 (2) of the Aircraft Rules, 1937 requires airlines to display established
tariff on their respective website with a 'single consolidated fare' and give its
complete break-up also for consumer's benefit.;
The airlines are mandated to display the Tariff Sheet at a prominent location on
the home page of airlines' website.;
The airfares are dynamic in nature and follow the principle of demand and
supply. The trends in airfare prices in India exhibit considerable seasonality,
prevailing fuel price, the capacity of the aircraft operating on the route,
competition on the sector, season, holidays, festivals, long weekends, events
(sports, fairs, contests) etc. The months of May and June experience heightened
traffic, with a notable peak in international travel commencing in mid-July, which
concurrently influences domestic demand. From July to September, there is
typically a decline in travel activity due to the monsoon season. Nevertheless,
the arrival of the festive season in October, particularly during the celebration of
Diwali, triggers a notable resurgence in travel demand. By mid-January, travel
demand begins to wane, continuing its decline until the final week of April.
Following this period, a renewed interest in travel coincides with the summer
holidays, contributing to an increase in demand once again.;
Besides this, the pricing of airfares is significantly influenced by operational
constraints at airports. Routes experiencing high tourist demand, are subject to
limitations imposed by terrain, weather conditions, and restricted operating
hours. The combination of constrained capacity and elevated demand leads to
fluctuations in airfares.;
1 3.02.2025 259
Airfares are not subject to regulation by the Government and airlines have the
flexibility to determine their airfares based on their operational needs, while
adhering to Rule 135 of the Aircraft Rules, 1937. While the government generally
refrains from regulating airfares to maintain market competitiveness, however, it
remains vigilant, and the Government intervenes to shift capacity from one
sector to another to prevent exorbitant pricing to ensure passenger comfort and
welfare.;
(e): There is no such proposal for regulation of Airfare by AERA.
(g): The details of amount paid to airlines under Viability Gap Funding, year
and airline wise for last 3 years is given in the enclosed Statement.
1 3.02.2025 260

(ग) िबहार के उन िजल(cid:332) का (cid:202) यौरा (cid:179) या है जहां उ(cid:179) त योजना के अंतग(cid:170)त अब तक पीएनजी कने(cid:179) शन
(cid:255)दान िकए गए ह(cid:167)?
पे(cid:367)ोिलयम और (cid:255)ाकृ ितक गैस म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा पय(cid:170)टन म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी
सुरेश गोपी) :
(क) और (ख): पाइ(cid:200) ड (cid:255)ाकृितक गैस (पीएनजी) कन(cid:179)े शन उपल(cid:202) ध कराना नगर गैस िवतरण
(सीजीडी) नेटवक(cid:170) के िवकास का भाग है और यह काय(cid:170) प(cid:367)े ोिलयम और (cid:255)ाकृितक गैस िविनयामक
बोड(cid:170) (पीएनजीआरबी) (cid:300)ारा (cid:255)ािधकृत कं पिनय(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा िकया जाता है। वाि(cid:208) मक(cid:236) नगर संसदीय

अिनवाय(cid:170) प(cid:229)रयोजना िनयोजन

प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंको स(cid:335)पना

नह(cid:233), तो इसके (cid:179)या कारण ह(cid:167);

योजना ह,ै िजसके अंतग(cid:170)त अिधक गंत(cid:211)य(cid:332) और माग(cid:334) को शािमल करने के िलए समय-समय पर बोली

िमलावटी देसी घी का (cid:211) यापार
1417. (cid:174)ी अ(cid:321)ण गोिवल:
(cid:179) या खा(cid:299) (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण उ(cid:299)ोग म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:
(क) (cid:179) या सरकार को िमलावटी दसे ी घी के (cid:211) यापार क(cid:236) जानकारी है; और
(ख) यिद हां, तो सरकार (cid:300)ारा इसे रोकने के िलए क(cid:236) जा रही कार(cid:170)वाई का (cid:202) यौरा (cid:179) या है?
खा(cid:299) (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण उ(cid:299)ोग म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा रले म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी रवनीत
िसहं ):
(क) और (ख) भारतीय खा(cid:299) सरु (cid:177)ा एवं मानक (cid:255)ािधकरण (एफएसएसएआई) क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना खा(cid:299)
सरु (cid:177)ा एवं मानक अिधिनयम 2006 (अथा(cid:170)त एफएसएस अिधिनयम 2006) म (cid:164) िनिहत (cid:255)ावधान(cid:332) के
अनसु ार (cid:214)वा(cid:214)(cid:195)य एवं प(cid:229)रवार क(cid:208)याण म(cid:253)ं ालय के अंतग(cid:170)त क(cid:236) गई थी और इसे खा(cid:299) पदाथ(cid:334) के
िलए िव(cid:178)ान आधा(cid:229)रत मानक िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत करने तथा मानव उपभोग के िलए सुरि(cid:177)त एवं पौि(cid:310)क
भोजन क(cid:236) उपल(cid:202)धता सिु नि(cid:306)त करने के िलए उनके िविनमा(cid:170)ण, भंडारण, िवतरण, िब(cid:248)(cid:236) और
आयात को िविनयिमत करने का अिधदेश िदया गया है।
1 3.02.2025 277
एफएसएसएआई (cid:300)ारा साझा क(cid:236) गई जानकारी के अनुसार , वे देश भर म(cid:164) उपभो(cid:265)ाओ ंको
घी सिहत सरु ि(cid:177)त खा(cid:299) उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) क(cid:236) उपल(cid:202)धता सिु नि(cid:306)त करने के िलए पूरी तरह (cid:255)ितब(cid:294) ह(cid:167)।
तदनसु ार, उ(cid:198)ह(cid:332)ने खा(cid:299) सुर(cid:177)ा और मानक (खा(cid:299) उ(cid:194)पाद मानक और खा(cid:299) अड्िडिटव) िविनयम,
2011 के उप-िविनयम 2.1.8 के तहत 'दूध वसा उ(cid:194)पाद (घी)' के मानक िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत िकए ह(cid:167), िजसम(cid:164)
घी क(cid:236) (cid:255)ामािणकता क(cid:236) जाचँ के िलए फै टी एिसड संरचना का उ(cid:208)लेख िकया गया है। िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत सरु (cid:177)ा
और गणु व(cid:176)ा मापदडं (cid:332) के िव(cid:308)ेषण को सुिवधाजनक बनाने के िलए, एफएसएसएआई ने िव(cid:308)ेषण के
तरीक(cid:332) का मैनुअल भी जारी िकया है।
इसके अलावा, संबंिधत रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के खा(cid:299) सरु (cid:177)ा िवभाग(cid:332) और एफएसएसएआई
के (cid:177)े(cid:253)ीय काया(cid:170)लय(cid:332) के अिधकारी पूर े वष(cid:170) िनयिमत िनगरानी, मॉनीट(cid:229)रंग, िनरी(cid:177)ण और या(cid:320)ि(cid:184)छक
नमूनाकरण करते ह(cid:167)। ऐसे मामल(cid:332) म(cid:164) जहां उपरो(cid:265) िविनयम(cid:332) म(cid:164) िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत वैधािनक आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंका
गैर-अनपु ालन पाया जाता है, दोषी खा(cid:299) (cid:211)यवसाय संचालक(cid:332) (एफ़बीओ) के िखलाफ दडं ा(cid:194)मक
कार(cid:170)वाई शु(cid:322) क(cid:236) जाती है।

to state:

(a) the total length in kilometres completed and awarded under Bharatmala
Pariyojana as of December 2024, along with the project cost;
(b) the specific components of Bharatmala Pariyojana, such as Economic
Corridor, Border and International Connectivity, and Coastal and Port
Connectivity Roads, that have been prioritized, State-wise;
(c) the expected socio-economic impact of Bharatmala Pariyojana, as
assessed in the IIM, Bangalore study and the details of any preliminary
findings thereon;
(d) the manner in which this project contributes to reducing logistics costs and
improving national connectivity; and
(e) the details of the expected timelines for completion of the remaining
sections under this initiative?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN
JAIRAM GADKARI):
(a) Bharatmala Pariyojana was approved by Government in 2017 covering a
length of 34,800 km across the country. As on 31.12.2024, projects with a capital
cost of Rs.8.54 lakh crore covering a total length of 26,425 km have been
awarded and out of this, 19,201 km has already been constructed under
Bharatmala Pariyojana.
1 3.02.2025 279
(b) The various components of awarded projects under Bharatmala
Pariyojana are as below:
Awarded

roads

(c) The preliminary findings of study conducted by IIM, Bangalore to evaluate
the impact of Bharatmala Pariyojana indicates positive effects across multiple
sectors. Economically, household income and expenditure have increased,
along with a rise in car sales. Logistics efficiency has improved with reduced
1 3.02.2025 280
costs. Businesses have benefited from better access to markets and factories.
Socially, travel time to schools and healthcare facilities has decreased,
enhancing accessibility. Overall, the study suggests that Bharatmala Pariyojana
has significantly contributed to socio-economic growth.
(d) Bharatmala Pariyojana envisaged improvement in the logistics efficiency
and connectivity in the country including connectivity to tribal, aspirational and
LWE affected districts. Further, development of corridors will reduce the travel
time substantially between key economic centers.
(e) The projects under implementation are targeted for completion by financial
year 2027-28.
SETTING UP OF INFRASTRUCTURE TO MITIGATE FLOODING
1419. SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
SHRIMATI D. K. ARUNA:
SHRI EATALA RAJENDER:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government proposes to assist in setting up of sump and pump
arrangements with shutters and additional infrastructure in drains and
canals across various cities of the country, spending Rs.100 crore to
mitigate flooding in various parts of the cities, if so, the details thereof,
State/UT-wise, particularly in Andhra Pradesh and Telangana;
1 3.02.2025 281
(b) whether the Government/States/Corporations are required to identify
locations that require additional infrastructure to prevent flooding along
canals and drains and if so, whether civic officials will study the flood-prone
areas near canals and hold consultations with residents;
(c) if so, the details thereof and the present status along with the funds
sanctioned/spent during the last five years, State/UT-wise, particularly in
Andhra Pradesh and Telangana; and
(d) whether any specific outcomes or improvements have been observed in
the flood-prone areas due to these efforts and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (d) Management of urban flooding falls under the purview of the State
Governments and the Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)/ Urban Development
Authorities, who are responsible for maintaining the drainage and sewerage
system. Government of India supplements the efforts of the States through
schematic interventions/ advisories. It provides financial and technical support to
the States to strengthen Urban Planning ecosystem.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) is implementing Atal Mission
for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT), under which storm water
drainage is an admissible component which involves construction and
improvement of drains/ storm water drains in order to reduce and eliminate
flooding. Under the scheme, States/Union Territories are empowered to select,
1 3.02.2025 282
prioritise, design, and implement the projects within the broad framework of
AMRUT guidelines. Under AMRUT, 840 Storm Water Drainage projects worth
₹3,015.96 crore has been approved. As reported by the States, 793 Storm Water
Drainage projects worth ₹2,322.8 crore have been completed under AMRUT. This
has resulted in elimination of 3,678 water logging points
Under AMRUT, 4 drainage projects worth ₹100.81 crore have been
completed in Andhra Pradesh which has resulted in elimination of 6 water logging
points. Telangana has not taken up any drainage project under AMRUT.
Under AMRUT 2.0, rejuvenation of water bodies and wells is one of the main
components. The admissible elements under this include harvesting the rainwater
through storm water drains into water body (which is not receiving sewage/
effluent). Under AMRUT 2.0, 3,032 water body rejuvenation projects worth
₹6,214.15 crore have been approved so far which includes 196 projects worth
₹522.04 crore in Andhra Pradesh and 142 projects worth ₹380.11 crore in
Telangana.
MoHUA has also published the following documents/advisory guidelines for
improving urban drainage and flood management, viz.:
(i) Urban and Regional Development Plans Formulation and Implementation
(URDPFI) Guidelines, 2014
(https://mohua.gov.in/upload/uploadfiles/files/URDPFI%20Guidelines%20Vol%20
I(2).pdf)
1 3.02.2025 283
(ii) Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for Urban Flooding
((https://mohua.gov.in/upload/uploadfiles/files/SOP%20Urban%20flooding_5%20
May%202017.pdf)
(iii) River Centric Urban Planning Guidelines in 2021 to enable cities in the
development of conjunctive water management approaches including nature-
based solution
(https://mohua.gov.in/upload/uploadfiles/files/RCUP%20Guidelines.pdf)
(iv) Guidance Document on Creation of Rain Water Harvesting Parks
(https://mohua.gov.in/pdf/6566e1048ab41guidance-document-on-rainwater-
harvesting-parks-final.pdf)
SHORTAGE OF CNG PUMPS
1420. SHRI SHRIRANG APPA CHANDU BARNE:
SHRIMATI BHARTI PARDHI:
SHRI DHARMENDRA YADAV:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) whether there has been an increase in the number of CNG-operated vehicles
across the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether there has been a shortage of CNG Pumps in comparison with the
increased number of CNG-operated vehicles across the country;
(d) if so, the details thereof;
1 3.02.2025 284
(e) whether there is a proposal to establish new CNG Pumps in each district
across the country, if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
(f) the details of the total number of CNG Pumps established across the country,
State/UTwise, specially in Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh till
date?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
(a) and (b): The year-wise details of the registration of CNG vehicles are given
in the enclosed Statement-I.
(c) and (d): Establishment of CNG stations is part of development of City Gas
Distribution (CGD) Networks and the same is carried out by the entities
authorized by Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB) as per
their Minimum Work Programme (MWP). As per the MWP, the authorized
entities are committed to establish 18,336 CNG stations across the country by
2034. As on 30.11.2024, the entities have established 7,395 CNG Stations
against the pro-rata target of establishing 4,025 CNG stations. The cumulative
number of CNG Stations established in last three years in the country is as
below: -
Year March, 2022 March, 2023 March, 2024
CNG Stations 4433 5665 6861
(Nos.)
(Source: PNGRB)
1 3.02.2025 285
(e) and (f): After completion of 12/12A CGD bidding round, PNGRB has
authorized 307 Geographical Areas (GAs) covering almost 100% of the
mainland area of the country for the development of CGD network. The details
of CNG Stations established across the country including in the state of
Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh as on 30.11.2024, are given
in the enclosed Statement-II.

PROJECTS UNDER AMRUT 2.0 IN ANDHRA PRADESH
1421. DR. C. M. RAMESH:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to
state:
(a) the number of projects sanctioned under AMRUT 2.0 in the district of
Anakapalli in Andhra Pradesh;
(b) the details of the projects sanctioned, tenders awarded and completed;
(c) the current status of each of the sanctioned projects; and
(d) the time by which all the projects under AMRUT 2.0 are likely to be completed?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (d) Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT) 2.0
was launched on 01 October 2021. Under AMRUT 2.0, 5 projects worth ₹24.85
crore (including Operations and Maintenance cost) have been approved by the
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) in Anakapalli district of Andhra
Pradesh, which includes 3 water supply projects worth ₹22.56 crore and 2 water
body rejuvenation projects worth ₹2.29 crore. The details of the projects are
given in the enclosed Statement.
As reported by State of Andhra Pradesh, contracts for 3 projects have
been awarded and Notice Inviting Tender (NIT) have been issued for another 2
projects. 2 water body rejuvenation projects have been completed and the
estimated date of completion of remaining 3 water supply projects is September,
2026.

UNDERUTILIZATION OF FUNDS BY NPTI
1423. SHRI KIRTI AZAD:
Will the Minister of POWER be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the reasons for sub-optimal utilization of budgetary allocations
by the National Power Training Institute (NPTI) during the years 2022-23 and
2023-24;
(b) the details of the specific steps taken/being taken by the Government to
address the challenges responsible for the underutilization of allocated funds
and to enhance the absorptive capacity of the said Institute;
(c) whether the Government has assessed the factors that led to National Power
Training Institute (NPTI) losing its self-sufficient status in 2018 and if so, the
details thereof;
(d) the details of the measures being implemented to help the said Institute to
restore its financial self-sufficiency and if so, the details thereof; and
(e) the details of any long-term strategy to strengthen the said Institution's
operational and financial performance to ensure its effective contribution to the
power sector?
1 3.02.2025 295
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF POWER; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF NEW AND RENEWABLE ENERGY (SHRI
SHRIPAD YESSO NAIK):
(a) and (b) The Govt. of India provide funds to National Power Training
Institute (NPTI) for meeting the requirement of its pension liabilities and for
creation/ upgradation of Infrastructure in its institutes spread across the
country. In FY 2022-23 as against budgetary allocation of Rs.50 Cr, NPTI
utilized Rs. 14.35 Cr out of which Rs. 12 Cr was utilized for meeting pension
liabilities and Rs. 2.35 Cr for Infrastructure upgradation. Similarly in FY 2023-
24 as against budgetary allocation of Rs. 35 Cr, NPTI utilized Rs. 22.93 Cr out
of which an amount of Rs. 10 Cr was utilized for meeting pension liabilities and
Rs. 12.93 Cr for Infrastructure upgradation. During the above mentioned
period, NPTI proposed various works related to the upgradation of critical
infrastructure of its institutes like construction and furnishing of hostel building
etc. Due to considerable time taken by the institute in preparation and
subsequent approval of Detailed Project Report (DPRs), the proposed works
under them could not be executed and funds allocated to NPTI remained
unutilized.
To enhance the absorptive capacity of the institution, the institute was
advised by MoP to submit proposals well in advance along with utilization
certificates regularly so that funds could be released in time. With the help of
these measures the utilization of funds under infrastructure head has improved
in FY 2023-24 compared to FY 2022-23.
1 3.02.2025 296
(c) and (d) NPTI is an autonomous institute registered under the Haryana
Registration and Regulation of Societies Act of 2012. It has consistently
maintained self-sufficiency since 2004-05 and is bearing all recurring and non-
plan expenditures, including the salaries of its employees, solely through its
own revenue earnings. As mentioned above, the financial support of Ministry
is limited to provide Grants for infrastructure development and towards pension
liabilities.
(e) Following steps are taken to strengthen NPTI’s operational and financial
performance:
i. Ministry of Power, Govt. of India designated NPTI as a National Apex body for
fulfilling the training and Human Resource Development requirements of the
power sector in the country.
ii. NPTI is conducting various Long-Term, Medium-Term, Short-Term,
Specialized Training Programs for Engineers and Supervisors in Thermal
Power Plant Engineering , Hydro Power, Transmission/Sub-Transmission
/Distribution, Smart Grid Technologies, Power System Operations,
Regulations for Transmission and Distribution utilities/ Generating plant both
conventional and Renewable Energy Power plants, Large RE Grid Integration
technologies, Hybrid Energy such as Solar, Wind, Biomass, Co-generation etc.
The institute has progressively increased the number of such training
programmes over the years.
iii. NPTI is also conducting Capacity Building Programs like Mandatory
Foundation Program, Awareness Program under National Mission on use of
1 3.02.2025 297
Biomass in Thermal Power Plants, Training and Certification Programs on
Cyber Security, Training Program under Revamped Distribution Sector
Scheme (RDSS), System Operator Certification Examination, System
Operator Training Program Training Programs under PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli
Yojana etc.
iv. Foundation and Mid-Career training programmes have been started for career
advancement of the Central Power Engineering Service officers of CEA.
Induction Training for various utilities such as DVC, NHPC, POSOCO,
MahaGenco, MPGenco etc. are being conducted.
v. Various long term programs are currently running like MBA, PGDC and PDC
in various streams such as MBA in Power Management, Post Graduate
Diploma Course (PGDC) in Power Plant Engineering, Renewable Energy and
Grid Interface Technologies Power Distribution and Emerging Technologies,
Hydro Power Plant Engineering and Post Diploma Course (PDC) in Power
Plant Engineering.
vi. An AR/VR lab to train personnels in understanding critical operation of thermal
power plants has been proposed for development at Durgapur centre with the
financial support of DVC.
vii. An E-Learning Management System (LMS) was developed to facilitate online
certification courses.
viii. NPTI conducts Training of Trainers (ToT) programs for its regular employees
and faculties for enhancing their skills and also allows employee to be part of
the Specialized Training Programs for Utilities, International
1 3.02.2025 298
workshop/Seminar and conferences conducted by other centres of NPTI and
by other organizations.
ix. NPTI is also engaged in providing consultancy services to various utilities.
x. To facilitate more number of trainings, infrastructure have been strengthened,
under which 02 new Institutes at Alappuzha in Kerala and Shivpuri in Madhya
Pradesh have been established and started functioning from Year 2020
onwards. Further, scheme for Development of the Critical infrastructure (Hostel
upgradation and other Civil works) of 09 Institutes of NPTI, and establishment
of three new Hostel at NPTI institutes were approved.
xi. To improve quality of training, 06 Nos. of new Multifunctional Simulator have
been commissioned successfully at NPTI-PSTI, Bengaluru, NPTI (ER),
Durgapur, NPTI, CO, Faridabad, NPTI- Alappuzha, Kerala, NPTI (WR), Nagpur
and NPTI, Shivpuri.
ONE DISTRICT ONE PRODUCT SCHEME
1424. SHRI PRAVEEN PATEL:
SHRIMATI MALA RAJYA LAXMI SHAH:
SHRI MANISH JAISWAL:
DR. HEMANT VISHNU SAVARA:
SHRI DHARAMBIR SINGH:
SHRI NABA CHARAN MAJHI:
1 3.02.2025 299
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to
state:
(a) the manner in which the Government ensure that the One District One
Product approach is effectively integrated across all the districts;
(b) whether there is any provision for providing export support to the micro food
processing enterprises;
(c) if so, the details thereof including the details of Palghar district of
Maharashtra;
(d) whether the Government proposes to extend the scope or budget of the said
scheme
beyond the financial year 2025-26; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) Ministry of Food Processing Industries through Centrally-Sponsored
Pradhan Mantri Formalisation of Micro food processing Enterprises (PMFME)
Scheme is implementing One District One Product (ODOP) approach to reap
the benefit of scale in terms of procurement of inputs, availing common services
and marketing of products. ODOP for the scheme provides the framework for
value chain development and alignment of support infrastructure. As on 31st
1 3.02.2025 300
January 2025, ODOP have been approved for 726 Districts across 35 States/
UTs under the PMFME Scheme. The ODOP is approved on the basis of
recommendation by the States and in consultation with Ministry of Agriculture.
(b) and (c) District as Export Hub (DEH) Initiative is implemented by Directorate
General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) under Ministry of Commerce and Industries
wherein support is provided for certification, branding, and direct market linkages
with international buyers. Also, Ministry is working in coordination with
Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority
(APEDA) to provide incentives to processed food exporters including micro food
processors under its different schemes for Infrastructure, Quality, Market
Development etc. These schemes are implemented across the country,
including Palghar district of Maharashtra. Till 31st January 2025, loans for
1,22,512 micro food processing enterprises have been sanctioned out of which
282 micro food processing units have been sanctioned in the Palghar district of
Maharashtra.
(d) and (e) As of now, there is no such proposal.
CHALLENGES IN IMPLEMENTATION OF HAR GHAR NAL SE JAL
YOJANA IN RURAL AREAS
1425. DR. NAMDEO KIRSAN :
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the number of households covered under Har Ghar Nal Se Jal Yojana in
Maharashtra, district-wise;
1 3.02.2025 301
(b) whether any study has been conducted by the Government to identify
challenges in implementing the said scheme, especially in rural areas due to its
slow progress;
(c) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor;
(d) whether the Government has any long-term plans for increasing coverage of
households under the said scheme in Maharashtra; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS (SHRI V.
SOMANNA):
(a) Since August 2019, Government of India in partnership with States is
implementing Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal to make provision of
potable water to every rural household of the country, through functional tap
water connection i.e. at a service level of 55 litre per capita per day (lpcd), of
prescribed quality (BIS:10500), on regular and long-term basis.
As reported by the State Government of Maharashtra, at the time of
launch of JJM in the State on 15.08.2019, only 48.44 lakh rural households had
tap water connections. Since then, 81.16 lakh additional rural households have
been provided with tap water connections. Thus, as on 10.02.2025, out of
146.81 lakh rural households in the state, the provision of tap water supply is
available to more than 129.59 lakhs (88.28%) rural households. The district-wise
details of households with tap water connection in Maharashtra is given in the
enclosed Statement.
1 3.02.2025 302
(b) and (c) : To expedite the planning and implementation, as well as monitoring
and handhold States/ UTs including Maharashtra, Government of India has
taken number of steps which inter alia includes discussion and finalization of
annual action plan (AAP) in consultation with States/ UTs, regular review of
planning and implementation, workshops/ conferences/ webinars for capacity
building and knowledge sharing, field visits by multi-disciplinary team to provide
technical support, etc. To bring transparency and effective monitoring, an online
‘JJM dashboard’ has been created, which provides State/ UT, district and
village-wise progress as well as status of provision of tap water supply to rural
homes.
Under JJM, Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation undertakes annual
assessment of the functionality of household tap water connections provided
under the Mission, through an independent third-party agency, based on
standard statistical sampling. During the functionality assessment 2022, in
Maharashtra, it was found that 93% of households (HHs) had working tap
connections. Out of these, 68% were getting water in adequate quantity, 75%
were getting water regularly as per the schedule of water supply for their piped
water supply scheme, and 81% of households were receiving water as per the
prescribed water quality standards.
States, including Maharashtra have informed that lack of dependable drinking
water sources in water-stressed, drought prone and desert areas, presence of
geo-genic contaminants in ground water, uneven geographical terrain, scattered
rural habitations, delay in release of the matching State share in some States,
1 3.02.2025 303
lack of technical capacity with implementing agencies, Gram Panchayats and
local communities to plan, manage, operate and maintain the water supply
schemes, rising price of raw materials, delay in obtaining statutory/ other
clearances, etc. are a few of the problems being faced in the implementation of
the Mission. Further, CoVID-19 pandemic and poor availability of raw materials
especially DI/ HDPE pipes due to Russia-Ukraine military conflict have also
impacted the pace of implementation in the States.
(d) and (e) As reported by the Government of Maharashtra, the State is
planning for saturation by March, 2026.

SABARIMALA INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT
1427. SHRI HIBI EDEN:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) the current status of the completion of the Sabarimala International Airport in
Kerala;
(b) whether the Government has taken any steps during the last one year
regarding the completion of the said airport; and
(c) if so, the details thereof along with the current status of the land acquisition
process for the purpose?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): Government of Kerala (GoK) envisaged the development of a
Greenfield Airport in Kottayam (Sabrimala) near Erumeli in Kottayam district for
which the Ministry of Civil Aviation, Government of India (GoI), has already
granted "Site Clearance" in April 2023, under the Greenfield Airports (GFA)
Policy 2008. Further, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change,
GoI has also granted Terms of Reference (ToR) to the Project in July 2023 and
State Government has conducted the Environment Impact Assessment (EIA)
Study.;
As per the GFA Policy, the responsibility for implementation of the airport
project including land acquisition, funding, RandR etc. lies with the Project
1 3.02.2025 320
Proponent concerned, i.e. Kerala State Industrial Development Corporation
Limited (KSIDC), a State Government of Kerala Agency. KSIDC is required to
prepare a Detailed Project Report (DPR) including details like development plan,
project cost and funding, traffic projections, timelines for completion of the
project etc. As per GFA Policy, 2008 once completed, this report is required to
be submitted along with an application to GoI for grant of "In Principle" approval,
before commencement of the development works.
DEVELOPMENT OF REGIONAL CONNECTIVITY IN ASPIRATIONAL
DISTRICTS
1428. SHRI VISHNU DAYAL RAM :
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government is prioritising regional connectivity in aspirational
districts, including Palamu and Garhwa under the UDAN scheme and if so, the
details thereof;
(b) the number of unserved airports identified for development in the said
districts;
(c) the funds allocated for promoting regional air connectivity in underdeveloped
regions during the last three years; and
(d) the measures being undertaken by the Government to improve air
connectivity and boost economic development in such areas?
1 3.02.2025 321
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (d) The Government launched the Regional Connectivity Scheme-Ude
Desh ka Aam Nagrik (RCS - UDAN) in October 2016 to enhance regional air
connectivity from unserved and underserved airports in the country for making
air travel affordable to the people of India including the State of Jharkhand. The
government has recently announced launching of modified UDAN Scheme to
support helipads in aspirational districts.
Under RCS-UDAN , six (06) airports namely, Bokaro, Dumka, Jamshedpur,
Hazaribagh, Deoghar and Daltonganj in the state of Jharkhand have been
identified for development and operation of RCS flights.;
For revival and development of un-served and under-served
airports/heliports/water-aerodromes in the country from FY 2021-22 to FY 2023-
24 Rs. 2418 Cr. were allocated. ;
LPG CONNECTIONS TO ELIGIBLE BENEFICIARIES UNDER PMUY
1429. SHRIMATI MALA RAJYA LAXMI SHAH:
SHRI BHOJRAJ NAG:
SHRI ALOK SHARMA:
SHRI JASWANTSINH SUMANBHAI BHABHOR:
DR. RAJESH MISHRA:
1 3.02.2025 322
SHRI RAJKUMAR CHAHAR:
SHRI NABA CHARAN MAJHI:
SHRI MANISH JAISWAL:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to
state:
(a) whether any measures have been taken by the Government to ensure that
all eligible beneficiaries under the Socio-Economic, Caste Census list and
specified categories become aware of the Pradhan Mantri Ujjawala Yojana
(PMUY);
(b) the mechanism available to ensure sustained use of LPG by the beneficiaries
instead of reverting to conventional fuels;
(c) whether the Government proposes to expand PMUY to include additional
subsidy for refill cost to ensure sustainability in use of LPG by poor households;
(d) if so, the details thereof;
(e) the total number of eligible beneficiaries identified for LPG connections and
were distributed to them so far under the said scheme in Sidhi Parliamentary
Constituency; and
(f) the time by which the LPG connections would be distributed to the remaining
households?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND
NATURAL GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM
(SHRI SURESH GOPI):
1 3.02.2025 323
(a) to (f): Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) was launched in May, 2016
with an objective to provide deposit free LPG connection to adult women from
poor households across the country. LPG connection under PMUY is released
in the name of adult women of the poor households, provided no LPG
connection exists in the name of any family member of the household and
fulfilling other terms and conditions. Households belonging to Socio Economic
Caste Census (SECC) list or from seven other categories such as Scheduled
Caste(SC) Households, Scheduled Tribe(ST) Households, Most Backward
Classes (MBC), Beneficiaries of PM Awas Yojana (Gramin), Beneficiaries of
Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY), Forest Dwellers, Resident of islands/ river
islands, Tea garden / ex-tea garden workers or poor households not covered
under the above categories by submitting 14 point declaration are eligible for a
PMUY connection. Under Ujjwala 2.0, a special provision has been made for
migrant families who can use a self-declaration instead of Proof of Address and
Ration Card to apply for a PMUY connection.
The target to release 8 crore connections under PMUY was achieved in
September 2019. To cover the remaining poor households, Ujjwala 2.0 was
launched in August 2021 with a target to release 1 crore additional PMUY
connections, which was achieved in January 2022. Subsequently, the
Government decided to release 60 lakh more LPG connections under Ujjwala
2.0 and the target of 1.60 crore Ujjwala 2.0 connections has been achieved
during December 2022. Further, Government approved release of additional 75
1 3.02.2025 324
lakh connections under PMUY Scheme for the period FY 2023-24 to 2025-26
which has already been achieved during July 2024. As on 01.01.2025, there are
10.33 crore LPG connections under PMUY scheme. Details of PMUY
connections in district covered under Sidhi Parliamentary Constituency are given
in the enclosed Statement.
LPG consumption of PMUY beneficiaries is monitored on regular basis.
Consumption of domestic LPG by households depends on several factors like
food habits, household size, cooking habits, tradition, flavor, taste, preferences,
price, availability of alternate fuels etc.
In order to improve access to LPG across the country, various steps have been
taken inter alia, including organizing campaigns for improving awareness about
PMUY, organizing melas/camps to enroll and distribute connections, promotion
through Out of Home (OOH) hoardings, radio jingles, Information, Education and
Communication (IEC) Vans etc., spreading awareness about advantages of
using LPG over other conventional fuels and safe usage of LPG through LPG
Panchayats, enrolment/awareness camps under Viksit Bharat Sankalp Yatra,
facilitation of consumers and their families for Aadhar enrolment and opening of
bank accounts for getting PMUY connections, simplification of process of getting
LPG connection, online application for PMUY connection at www.pmuy.gov.in,
nearest LPG distributors, Common Service Centres (CSC) etc., option of 5 Kg
Double Bottle Connection (DBC), swap option from 14.2 Kg to 5 Kg, provision
for Migrant Families to avail new connection on Self-Declaration instead of Proof
of Address and Ration Card. Further, OMCs are continuously commissioning
1 3.02.2025 325
new LPG Distributorships, especially in rural areas. Since the launch of PMUY
scheme, OMCs have commissioned 7944 Distributorships (commissioned
during 01.04.2016 to 31.10.2024) across the country, out of which 7361 (i.e. 93
%) are catering to rural areas. The steadily rising consumption of domestic LPG
by PMUY consumers indicative of growing acceptance of LPG by rural
households. Per Capita Consumption of PMUY households has increased from
3.01 refills (in terms of 14.2 Kg domestic LPG cylinders) in 2019-20 to 3.95 in
2023-24 to 4.4 (on annualised basis) in 2024-25 i.e. upto December 2024.
Since the launch of PMUY till financial year 2022-23, Government has been
bearing an expenditure of up to Rs. 1,600 per PMUY connection for Security
Deposit (SD) of Cylinder, Pressure Regulator, Suraksha Hose, DGCC booklet
and installation charges. From financial year 2023-24, this expenditure has been
increased to Rs. 2,200 per connection for 14.2 Kg Single Bottle Connection/ 5
Kg Double Bottle Connection and Rs. 1,300 per connection for 5 Kg Single Bottle
Connection.
To make LPG more affordable to PMUY consumers and ensure sustained usage
of LPG by them, Government started a targeted subsidy of Rs.200/- per 14.2 kg
cylinder for up to 12 refills per annum (and proportionately pro-rated for 5 Kg
connections) to the PMUY consumers in May 2022. In October 2023,
Government increased the targeted subsidy to Rs.300 per 14.2 kg cylinder for
up to 12 refills per annum (and proportionately pro-rated for 5 Kg connections).
After a targeted subsidy of Rs. 300/cylinder to PMUY consumers, Government
of India is providing 14.2 Kg LPG cylinders at an effective price of Rs.503 per
1 3.02.2025 326
cylinder (in Delhi). This is available to more than 10.33 crore Ujjwala
beneficiaries, across the country. No timeline has been set for distribution for
LPG connection to remaining households.
STATEMENT
Total connections released under PMUY
District
as on 01.01.2025 (lakh)
Sidhi 1.88 lakh
Singrauli 1.86 lakh
Shahdol 1.48 lakh
Source: Indian Oil Corporation on industry basis
1 3.02.2025 327
INFRASTRUCTURAL INADEQUACIES AT SHRI GURU RAM DASS JEE
INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT
1430. SHRI GURJEET SINGH AUJLA :
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government is aware of infrastructural inadequacies at Shri
Guru Ram Das Jee International Airport, including its handling capacity of only
1,960 passengers per hour along with inability to accommodate projected growth
in passenger traffic/flights and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the measures being undertaken by the Government to modernize/expand
the airport infrastructure;
(c) whether the Government is incorporating future projections for the next
twenty years into planning and if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government has received complaints regarding harassment of
passengers by ground handling staff and if so, the details thereof along with the
corrective measures taken in this regard;
(e) the steps being implemented to address exploitation of wheelchair-bound
passengers and to ensure their comfort during travel experience;
(f) whether the Government is investigating allegations of malpractices by
parking contractors, as overcharging/manipulating pick/drop areas and if so, the
details thereof along with the actions taken to address such exploitation; and
(g) whether the Government proposes to provide better facilities for CISF
personnel and their families by providing accommodations, recreational
1 3.02.2025 328
amenities and other essential services at Amritsar Airport and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) to (c): To address the increasing passenger traffic, the Airports Authority of
India (AAI) has undertaken the expansion and reconfiguration of the existing
terminal building at Amritsar Airport, adding approximately 10,000 square
meters to its area. Post-expansion, the terminal's peak hour passenger handling
capacity is expected to rise from 1600 to 2000 passengers.;
Furthermore, the expansion and upgradation of airports is a continuous process
and is undertaken by AAI and other Airport Operators, from time to time, based
on factors such as traffic demand, availability of land, commercial viability, socio-
economic considerations, and the willingness of airlines to operate to/from these
airports.
(d): No complaints regarding harassment of passengers by ground handling staff
at Amritsar Airport have been received by the Airports Authority of India.
However, to ensure prompt resolution of any passenger concerns, contact
numbers for the Duty Terminal Manager and Central Industrial Security Force
(CISF) Control Room are displayed at prominent places inside terminal building.
Additionally, dedicated help desk counters have been positioned at both the
departure and arrival terminals. ;
1 3.02.2025 329
(e): The Government has published Accessibility Standards and Guidelines for
Civil Aviation, which provide a comprehensive framework for educating and
guiding airport operators, airlines, security agencies, and other stakeholders in
the design, planning, and implementation of accessible infrastructure at airports.
These guidelines are aimed at ensuring that passengers with disabilities and
reduced mobility can move safely and independently within airport premises and
avail all facilities. The provisions include wheelchair assistance, buggy services,
accessible restrooms, adapted departure/arrival gates, dedicated check-in
counters, priority security screening, and designated assistance vehicle pick-
up/drop-off points.
(f): The Airports Authority of India (AAI) thoroughly investigates all complaints
against parking contractors regarding overcharging, manipulation of pick-
up/drop-off areas, and other irregularities. Appropriate corrective and punitive
measures are then implemented against the contractor in accordance with the
provisions of the concession agreement.
(g): AAI has provided bachelor accommodation, with all necessary amenities, at
Amritsar Airport for eligible CISF personnel, covering 55% of the sanctioned
strength. The remaining 45% of the personnel are availing House Rent
Allowance (HRA) as per Government of India rules.
PROPOSALS FROM MAHARASHTRA FOR HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS
1431. SHRI ANUP SANJAY DHOTRE:
1 3.02.2025 330
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Union Government has received any proposals from the State
Government of Maharashtra related to the Housing and Urban Affairs
sector;
(b) if so, the details thereof indicating the total number of proposals received,
approved and pending; and
(c) the time by which the Union Government proposes to approve the pending
proposals?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (c) As per the provisions of Article 243W of the Constitution, in conjunction
with the Seventh and Twelfth Schedules, matters relating to urban development
fall within the purview of States/Urban Local Bodies. However, Ministry of
Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) provides programmatic support to the
States/Union Territories (UTs) in their urban development agenda through its
various flagship Missions/Programmes viz. Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and
Urban Transformation (AMRUT) and AMRUT 2.0, Smart Cities Mission (SCM),
Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban (SBM-U) and SBM-U 2.0, Pradhan Mantri Awas
Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) and PMAY- U 2.0 and Urban Transport (UT) etc.
Details of the proposals received from Government of Maharashtra, approved
and pending in this regard under AMRUT, SBM-U, SCM and PMAY-U are given
in the enclosed Statement.
1 3.02.2025 331
STATEMENT
Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation:
Under AMRUT, State Annual Action Plans (SAAPs) worth ₹7,759.32 crore have
been approved for the State of Maharashtra. As reported by the State, 208
projects worth ₹8,013.28 crore taken up of which works worth ₹7,543.14 crore
have been physically completed. These projects include 43 water supply
projects worth ₹4,446.06 crore, 36 sewerage/septage management projects
worth ₹3,294.03 crore, one drainage project worth ₹94.06 crore and 128 green
spaces and park projects worth ₹179.13 crore.
Under AMRUT 2.0, 286 projects worth ₹30,553.59 crore have been approved by
this Ministry in Maharashtra State, which includes 117 water supply projects
worth ₹15,831.02 crore, 48 sewerage/septage management projects worth
₹12,992.75 crore, 96 water body rejuvenation projects worth ₹1,540.49 crore
and 25 green spaces and park projects worth ₹189.34 crore.
Swachh Bharat Mission – Urban:
Under this Mission, a total of five proposals having project cost of ₹ 6907.44
crores received from the State Govt. of Maharashtra under various components
and all the five proposals have been approved for release of centre share of ₹
2495.40 crores.
Smart Cities Mission (SCM): -
Under this Mission, eight Cities viz., Aurangabad, Kalyan-Dombivali, Nagpur,
Nashik, Pimpri-Chinchwad, Pune, Solapur and Thane have been selected from
1 3.02.2025 332
Maharashtra. A total of 348 projects amounting to ₹17,044 crores have been
taken, of which 324 projects amounting to ₹15,146 crore have been completed.
Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban:-
For the State of Maharashtra, a total of 13.64 lakh houses have been sanctioned,
out of which 11.60 lakh have been grounded for construction and 9.33 lakh has
been completed/delivered to the beneficiaries so far. The earlier scheme period
of 31.03.2022 has since been extended upto 31.12.2025 (except Credit Linked
Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) vertical of the scheme) to complete all the houses
sanctioned without changing the funding pattern and implementation
methodology. Presently, no proposal of the State of Maharashtra is pending
under this Mission.

INTEGRATED HOUSING AND SLUM DEVELOPMENT PROGRAMME
1433. SHRIMATI RACHNA BANERJEE:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of towns included in the Integrated Housing and Slum
Development Programme (IHSDP) in the country, State-wise including
West Bengal;
(b) the details of the works done under the said programme so far;
(c) the details of the success achieved in this regard so far; and
(d) the time by which the work under the said programme is likely to be
completed?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (d) Government of India launched Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban
Renewal Mission (JnNURM) in December, 2005 for assisting States/Urban
Territories (UTs) by extending Central Assistance for providing houses and
related civic amenities to urban poor including slum dwellers in 65 selected
cities under Basic Services to the Urban Poor (BSUP) and in other cities/towns
under Integrated Housing and Slum Development Programme (IHSDP)
including cities/towns of West Bengal. The period of JnNURM was upto March
2012. However, to complete the on-going projects sanctioned upto 31.03.2012,
the period was extended and funds were released upto March 2017. City-wise
1 3.02.2025 344
details of physical and financial progress under Integrated Housing and Slum
Development Programme (IHSDP) Component of JnNURM is given in the
enclosed Statement.
