HOUSING FOR HOMELESS PEOPLE
1583. SHRI SUKHDEO BHAGAT:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether it is a fact that the Annual Homelessness Assessment Report, 2024
shows that over 770,000 people were homeless during the specific count
conducted in January 2024, reflecting an 18% increase from the previous
year;
(b) if so, the reasons for the significant increase in homelessness despite the
Government’s efforts over the past decade; and
(c) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to address this issue and
reduce homelessness?
1 3.02.2025 992
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a)to (c): The report and data mentioned in question relates to United States of
America. The said report has no relation with India.
रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय राजमाग(cid:170)-9 पर दुघ(cid:170)टनाए ं
1584. (cid:174)ी कं वर िसहं तंवर:
(cid:179)या सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:
(क) (cid:179) या सरकार रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय राजमाग(cid:170)-9 पर उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश के हापुड बाबूगढ़ से िसखेड़ा, िह(cid:204)मतपुर और
िसंभावली तक िडवाइडर पर उगने वाली घास और राजमाग(cid:170) पर आवारा पशओु ं के कारण बढ़ती
दघु (cid:170)टनाओ ंसे अवगत है;
(ख) यिद हां, तो िडवाइडर(cid:332) से घास हटाने और आवारा पशुओ ंको राजमाग(cid:170) पर आने से रोकने के िलए
(cid:255)भावी उपाय नह(cid:233) करने के (cid:179) या कारण ह(cid:167);
(ग) िपछले तीन वष(cid:334) के दौरान रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय राजमाग(cid:170)-9 के इस खंड पर िकतनी दघु (cid:170)टनाए ं ह(cid:242)ई ह(cid:167) और इन
दघु (cid:170)टनाओ ंम(cid:164) िकतने लोग(cid:332) क(cid:236) म(cid:194)ृ य ु ह(cid:242)ई है और िकतने लोग घायल ह(cid:242)ए ह(cid:167);
(घ) सरकार (cid:300)ारा िडवाइडर(cid:332) से घास हटाने, आवारा पशओु ंको राजमाग(cid:170) पर आने से रोकने और रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय
राजमाग(cid:170)-9 के इस खंड पर सड़क सुर(cid:177)ा म(cid:164) सधु ार करने के िलए (cid:179) या कदम उठाए जा रहे ह(cid:167); और
(ङ) इन सरु (cid:177)ा उपाय(cid:332) को पूरा िकए जाने क(cid:236) समय-सीमा (cid:179) या है और इस उ(cid:293)(cid:212)े य के िलए िकतना बजट
आवंिटत िकया गया है?

1 3.02.2025 993

और रखरखाव के िलए उ(cid:194) तरदायी है। उ(cid:265) खंड, बाईपास िकए गए परु ाने िह(cid:214)से का भाग है, िजसे 2022

PROMOTION OF FOOD PROCESSING IN RURAL AND BACKWARD
REGIONS
1585. SHRIMATI BIJULI KALITA MEDHI:
Will the Minister of FOOD PROCESSING INDUSTRIES be pleased to state:
(a)whether there are any specific measures to promote food processing in rural and
backward regions of the country;
(b)the steps taken/being taken to develop cold storage facilities and reduce food
wastage across the supply chain; and
(c)whether there is any financial assistance or subsidy available for building cold
storage units, specifically for perishable crops like Darangiri bananas in the market
area?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FOOD PROCESSING
INDUSTRIES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS
(SHRI RAVNEET SINGH):
(a) In order to promote and ensure overall development of Food Processing
Industries, Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI) has been incentivizing
setting up/expansion of related infrastructure through its Central Sector Scheme
Pradhan Mantri Kisan SAMPADA Yojana (PMKSY), Production Linked Incentive
Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) and Centrally sponsored PM
Formalization of Micro Food Processing Enterprises (PMFME) scheme across the
1 3.02.2025 994
country including rural and backward regions.These schemes are not region or
state specific but demand driven.
Under PMKSY, credit linked financial assistance (capital subsidy) is provided to
entrepreneurs for setting up of food processing industries with total outlay of Rs
5520 Cr for 15th Finance Commission Cycle.
Under PMFME scheme, financial, technical and business support is provided for
setting up / upgradation of micro food processing enterprises. The scheme is
operational for a period of upto 2025-26 with an outlay of Rs.10,000 Crore.
PLISFPI is, inter alia, intended to support creation of global food manufacturing
champions and support Indian brands of food products in international market. The
Scheme is operational for a period from 2021-22 to 2026-27 with an outlay of Rs.
10,900 crores.
(b) and (c) Ministry of Food Processing Industries, under its schemes, does not
provide financial assistance for establishment of standalone cold storages facilities.
However, it incentivizes creation of cold chain, preservation and value addition
infrastructure as part of food processing projects under relevant component
schemes of PMKSY.
These schemes aim towards creation of modern infrastructure with efficient supply
chain management from farm gate to retail outlet which includes storage,
transportation, value addition, etc., thereby help in providing better returns to
farmers and creating huge employment opportunities, reducing wastage of
agricultural produce and increasing the processing level.
1 3.02.2025 995
Ministry extends financial support to prospective entrepreneurs for setting up of
different kinds of food processing industries including bananas processing, as per
respective scheme guidelines.
Department of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare is implementing Mission for
Integrated Development of Horticulture (MIDH) under which capital assistance is
provided for various horticulture activities including Construction/Expansion/
Modernization of cold storages of capacity up to 5000 MT in the country on the
basis of Annual Action Plan (AAP) received from States/UTs. AAPs are prepared
by the States/UTs based on their requirement, capacity and availability of
resources. The component of Cold Storage is demand/entrepreneur driven for
which Government assistance in the form of credit linked back ended subsidy is
available at the rate of 35% of the project cost in general areas and 50% of the
project cost in hilly and scheduled areas through respective State Horticulture
Missions. Under the scheme, assistance is available to individuals, Groups of
farmers/ growers/ consumers, Partnership/ Proprietary firms, Self Help Groups
(SHGs), Farmers Producer Organization (FPOs), Companies, Corporations,
Cooperatives, Cooperative Marketing Federations, Local bodies, Agricultural
Produce Market Committees (APMCs) and Marketing Boards and State
Governments.
Scheme also provides assistance for setting up of food processing units in North
East and Himalayan States only;. For Food Processing Units credit linked back
ended assistance @ 50% of eligible project cost, in North Eastern and Himalayan
States of the maximum project cost of Rs. 800.00 lakh/unit is available.”
1 3.02.2025 996
EFFECT OF DESTRUCTION OF TREES AND SOIL EROSION
1586. SHRI SUNIL BOSE:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Union Government has taken note of the fact that due to
indiscriminate destruction of trees and soil erosion, the silting of rivers have
increased and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether it has seriously affected the sources of water and if so, the details
thereof; and
(c) the efforts made/being made by the Government to prevent large scale
destruction of trees and soil erosion?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) to (c) Erosion, movement and deposition of sediment in river are natural
regulating functions of a river. Rivers tend to maintain a balance between the silt
load carried and silt load deposited, maintaining a river regime.
The protection of forests and tree resources is primarily the responsibility of
the State Governments/Union Territory Administrations. There are legal
frameworks for the protection of forests and tree resources which include, the
Indian Forest Act 1927, Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam 1980,
Wildlife (Protection) Act 1972, Biological Diversity Act, 2002 and the State Forest
Acts and Rules. The State Governments/UT Administrations take appropriate
actions to protect forests and trees under the provisions made under these
Acts/Rules. Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change also supports the
1 3.02.2025 997
efforts of the State Governments/ UT Administrations for afforestation and
conservation of forests through various schemes such as National Mission for
Green India, Nagar Van Yojana and "Mangrove Initiative for Shoreline Habitats and
Tangible Incomes (MISHTI)". Afforestation activities are also undertaken in a major
way by utilizing the "Compensatory Afforestation Fund (CAMPA)".
Further, the Flood management and anti-erosion schemes are formulated
and implemented by concerned State Governments as per their priority.
Government of India promotes and provides technical assistance, as well as
promotional financial assistance for critical areas. Union Government had
implemented Flood Management Programme (FMP) during XI and XII Plans for
providing central assistance to States for works related to flood control, anti-
erosion, drainage development, anti-sea erosion, etc. which subsequently
continued as a component of “Flood Management and Border Areas Programme
(FMBAP)” for the period from 2017-18 to 2020-21 and has further been extended
during 2021-22 to 2025-26.
For the comprehensive and holistic management of sediments in river
courses and water bodies, in a holistic manner, the Department of Water
Resources, River Development and Ganga Rejuvenation, Ministry of Jal Shakti, in
extensive consultations with Central Government Ministries/ Departments/ State
Governments/ UTs have prepared the “National Framework for Sediment
Management (NFSM)". Its emphasis is on reducing silt generation rather than silt
removal and promote technological innovations and best practices. The framework
1 3.02.2025 998
lays emphasis on sediment management through integrated river basin
management plan giving due consideration to environment and ecology.
STATUS OF PMAY-U IN SURAT
1587.SHRI MUKESH KUMAR CHANDRAKAANT DALAL:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the total financial allocations made for Surat district under the Pradhan
Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban (PMAY-U) since its inception;
(b) the current status of implementation of the PMAY-U in the said district,
including the number of completed and ongoing projects;
(c) the total number of beneficiaries availed the benefits under the PMAY-U in
Surat Parliamentary Constituency till date;
(d) the projected number of beneficiaries for the entirety of the PMAY-U based
on the current implementation trends and approved targets;
(e) whether there are any additional challenges related to financial allocations,
project delays, or beneficiary identification under the PMAY-U, particularly in Surat;
and
(f) if so, the steps taken/being taken by the Government to address them?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a)to(f) ‘Land’ and ‘Colonization’ are State subjects. Therefore, schemes related to
housing for their citizens are implemented by States/Union Territories (UTs).
However, the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairssupplements the efforts of
1 3.02.2025 999
States/Union Territories (UTs), by providing Central Assistance under Pradhan
Mantri Awas Yojana- Urban (PMAY-U) since 25.06.2015 to provide pucca houses
with basic civic amenities to eligible urban beneficiaries of Economically Weaker
Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG) and MiddleIncome Group (MIG)
categories across the country. The scheme period, has been extended upto
31.12.2025to complete all the houses sanctioned without changing the funding
pattern and implementation methodology.;
(b) PMAY-U is implemented through the States/UTs in the spirit of co-operative
federalism wherein participating States/UTs prepare project proposals in
consonance with the scheme guidelines, based on the demand for houses in urban
areas and submit to this Ministry for release of admissible Central Assistance.
(c) Based on the project proposals submitted by the State of Gujarat, in Surat
district a total of 2.06 lakh houses have been sanctioned with Central Assistance
of ₹ 4,398.25crore. Out of sanctioned houses 1.98 lakh houses have been
completed/delivered to the beneficiaries and ₹4,100.65 crore has been released till
date. Details of physical and financial progress for the State of Gujarat, Surat
district and Surat Parliamentary Constituency are given in the enclosed Statement.
Further, based on 9 years of experience and learning from implementing
PMAY-U, MoHUA has revamped the scheme and launched PMAY-U 2.0 ‘Housing
for All’ Mission with effect from 01.09.2024 for implementation in urban areas
across the country to construct, purchase and rent a house by 1 crore additional
eligible beneficiaries at affordable cost through four verticals i.e., Beneficiary Led
Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in Partnership (AHP), Affordable Rental
1 3.02.2025 1000
Housing (ARH) and Interest Subsidy Scheme (ISS). As on date, 29 States/UTs
including Gujarat have signed Memorandum of Agreement (MoA) to implement
PMAY-U 2.0 as per scheme guidelines. The operational guidelines of the PMAY-U
2.0 have been launched on 17.09.2024. The unified web portal for submitting online
application and operational guidelines can be accessed at https://pmay-
urban.gov.in.

AIRCRAFT OBJECTS BILL,2024
1590. DR. MALLU RAVI:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state:-
(a) whether the Government has introduced the Protection and Enforcement of
Interests in Aircraft Objects Bill, 2024 to enhance the leasing framework in the
aviation sector of the country and if so, the details thereof; and
(b) the key objectives of the said Bill and its expected impact on aircraft leasing
costs and air travel affordability for the passengers?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
(a) The Government has introduced the Protection of Interests in Aircraft Objects
Bill, 2025 in Parliament.;
(b) The bill is intended to provide for protection of interests in aircraft objects and
to implement the Convention on International Interests in Mobile Equipment and
1 3.02.2025 1007
the Protocol to the Convention on International Interests in Mobile Equipment on
Matters Specific to Aircraft Equipment, each signed at Cape Town on 16 November
2001 and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. This Bill implements
the Cape Town Treaty by statutorily recognising the insolvency remedies as
acceded to by India to protect interests in aircraft objects. The intended legislation
includes:
i. Provisions for Irrevocable De-registration and Export Request Authorization;
ii. The obligations of debtors and creditors towards the Registry Authority (the
Directorate General of Civil Aviation) for submission of dues and exercising
remedies in cases of default;
iii. overriding effect of the proposed Bill on inconsistent domestic laws and to protect
the power of Central Government, and other entities under domestic law to
arrest/detain an aircraft;
iv. confer jurisdiction upon the High Court as acceded to by India under the
Convention;
v. provisions such as delegated power to Central Government to make rules, for
removal of difficulties and to make, amend or withdraw declarations deposited
under the Cape Town Treaty.;
The expected impact of the proposed Bill includes:
i. Legal certainty with a primacy legislation which may reduce the risk premium,
lowering interest rates and lease rentals for financing/leasing of aircraft by
lessors/financiers leading to reduced leasing costs which is likely to be passed to
end users i.e. passenger/shipper;
1 3.02.2025 1008
ii. Better contract enforceability and repossession certainty would ease conducting
business in India and also give impetus for growth of domestic leasing hubs such
as in GIFT City.
STATUS OF SCM IN UTTAR PRADESH
1591.SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the status of Smart City Mission (SCM) in Uttar Pradesh along with the funds
allocated, released and utilised during the last three years and the current year till
31st January 2025, year and smart city-wise;
(b) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to address urban housing
amidst urbanization programmes; and
(c) whether the Government has introduced schemes or policies to increase
urban mobility and public transport reliability under SCM and if so, the details
thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) Under the Smart Cities Mission (SCM), ten cities, viz. Agra, Aligarh, Bareilly,
Jhansi, Kanpur, Lucknow, Moradabad, Prayagraj, Saharanpur and Varanasi have
been selected in the State of Uttar Pradesh. As on 20.01.2025, work orders have
been issued in 889 projects amounting to ₹21,104 crore, of which 863 projects (i.e.
97% of total projects) amounting to ₹20,371 crore have been completed and the
remaining 26 projects amounting to ₹733 crore are under implementation. The city
wise details of projects for these 10 cities are given in the enclosed Statement-I.
1 3.02.2025 1009
The city wise, year-wise details of allocation, release and utilization of central share
fund during the last three years and current year till 31st January 2025 is given in
the enclosed Statement-II.
(b) To address urban housing, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA)
supplements the efforts of the States/ Union Territories by providing central
financial assistance under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) since
June 25, 2015 with the aim of providing all-weather pucca houses equipped with
basic amenities to all eligible urban beneficiaries. The scheme operates through
four verticals- Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in
Partnership (AHP), In-Situ Slum Redevelopment (ISSR), and the Credit Linked
Subsidy Scheme (CLSS), as per the eligibility criteria outlined in the scheme
guidelines. Mission Period was up to 31.03.2022 which has been extended up to
31.12.2024 only to complete all the sanctioned houses without changing funding
pattern and implementation methodology of the scheme. CLSS vertical has not
been extended beyond 31.03.2022.
(c) Government of India’s policy and strategy for Indian Urban Development
follows the provisions as laid down in the Constitution of India. It is pertinent to
mention here that ‘Land’ and ‘Colonization’ are State subjects. Further, as per the
12th Schedule of Indian Constitution (Article 243W), Urban Planning including
Town Planning is the responsibility of Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)/ Urban
Development Authorities.
Government of India supplements the efforts of the State/UT Governments
through schematic interventions/ advisories and formulating policies, viz., National
1 3.02.2025 1010
Urban Transport Policy, 2006, Metro Rail Policy, 2017 and Transit Oriented
Development Policy, 2017, in order to plan and implement the urban transport
systems in most sustainable and viable manner.
Under Smart Cities Mission, 1,579 projects amounting to ₹ 37,304 crore (90%
of the total projects outlay) have been completed in urban mobility sector. These
projects range from smart roads, multilevel car parking, promotion of pedestrian
pathways, cycle tracks, Intelligent Traffic Management System etc.

TOLL PLAZAS
1592. DR. M. K. VISHNU PRASAD:
Will the Minister of ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the fact that numerous toll plazas are
functioningeven after the projects full cost is recovered and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the number of toll plazas in Tamil Nadu, Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh;
(c) the new toll plazas established in the said States during the last three years;
(d) the toll collected in toll plazas during the last five years in the said States; and
(e) the details of new toll plazas proposed to be set up in the said States?
THE MINISTER OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS (SHRI NITIN JAIRAM
GADKARI):
(a) Collection of user fee at fee plazas on National Highways is not related to the
recovery of project development cost. User fee is collected for the use of any
section of National Highway, in accordance with the provisions of the NH Fee
Rules.
As per the provision of National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and
Collection) Rules, 2008, the fee as notified as per Concession Agreement shall be
leviable till the end of the concession period and after the concession period is
over, the fee shall be collected by the Central Government or the executing
authority as per the fee specified under sub-rule (2) of rule 4 of National Highway
Fee Rules, 2008 on the date of transfer of such section of the National Highway,
bridge, tunnel or bypass, as the case may be, to be revised annually.
1 3.02.2025 1014
In respect of a public funded project, the fee leviable shall continue to be collected
in perpetuity for such section of the National Highway, bridge, tunnel or bypass, as
the case may be, to be revised annually in accordance with these rules.
In case of BOT Projects, after the end of Concession period, the Toll Plaza is
handed over to Government and thereafter the user fee is collected by the
Government through its implementing Agencies.
(b) The number of NH Fee Plazas in State of Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Tamil
Nadu, as on 31.01.2025, are as under:
Name of the state No. of operational user fee plazas
Tamil Nadu 70
Karnataka 58
Andhra Pradesh 72
(c) The number of new fee plazas started in last three years in the State of Andhra
Pradesh, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu are as under:
Name of the state No. of user fee plazas
Tamil Nadu 21
Karnataka 14
Andhra Pradesh 20
(d) The user fee collected at fee plazas on National Highways during the last five
years in the State of Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu are as under:
Total user fee collection (in Crore Rupees)
Name of the state

1 3.02.2025 1015

(e) The establishment of the user fee plazas and the commencement of user fee
collection depends upon the completion of the projects and the publication of the
Fee Notification in the Gazette of India. Upon completion of the project, user fee
collection will be commenced after notifying the applicable rates in the local
newspapers.

संभावना है?

(क) वत(cid:170)मान म(cid:164), इंिडगो कानपरु से ब(cid:164)गल(cid:321)ु , मुंबई, िद(cid:208)ली और हैदराबाद के िलए वािणि(cid:186)यक उड़ान(cid:164)

(cid:177)े(cid:253) पूरी तरह से िनय(cid:253)ं ण म(cid:265)ु हो गया है। एयरलाइन(cid:164) िकसी भी (cid:255)कार के िवमान को शािमल करने और

िवमानप(cid:176)न (cid:255)ािधकरण (एएआई) इस हवाई अड्डे पर एक िसिवल ए(cid:198)(cid:179)लेव का अनुर(cid:177)ण करता है।

िविश(cid:310) माग(cid:170)/शहर पर अपने घरले ू उड़ान काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म क(cid:236) योजना बनाती ह(cid:167), िजस पर एएआई का कोई

1 3.02.2025 1017
LEVEL OF NITRATE IN GROUNDWATER
1594. DR. DHARAMVIRA GANDHI:
SHRI SAPTAGIRI SANKAR ULAKA:
DR. PRASHANT YADAORAO PADOLE :
SHRI AMRINDER SINGH RAJA WARRING:
SHRI ANTO ANTONY:
SHRI GAURAV GOGOI:
DR. AMAR SINGH:
SHRI GURJEET SINGH AUJLA:
SHRI KULDEEP INDORA:
DR. KALYAN VAIJINATHRAO KALE:
SHRI K. SUDHAKARAN:
Will the Minister of JAL SHAKTI be pleased to state:
(a) the data on nitrate levels in groundwater in the country during the last five years,
districtwise including Sriganganagar and Hanumangarh districts of Rajasthan;
(b) the initiatives taken/being taken by the Government to reduce nitrate
contamination caused by agricultural practices, including the use of fertilisers and
their impact on water quality;
(c) the steps undertaken/being undertaken by the Government to mitigate
contamination from other sources, particularly in severely affected States like
Rajasthan, Punjab and Karnataka;
1 3.02.2025 1018
(d) the progress made in expanding the groundwater monitoring network, including
the use of digital devices to measure water levels and the expected outcomes of
increasing the network to 40,000 wells by 2027;
(e) the Government’s strategy to balance groundwater extraction with
replenishment, ensuring sustainable usage, particularly in overexploited regions;
and
(f) whether the Government has conducted any studies on the environmental and
public health impacts of chemical contamination in groundwater and if so, the
details and the outcomes thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF JAL SHAKTI (DR. RAJ
BHUSHAN CHOUDHARY):
(a) Central Ground Water Board (CGWB) generates ground water quality data
on a regional scale as part of its ground water quality monitoring program annually
and for various other scientific studies. The district-wise data on nitrate levels in
ground water samples recorded for the years 2019 and 2023 is available at the
following link:
https://cgwb.gov.in/sites/default/files/inlinefiles/percentage_of_samples_nitrate_m
orethan_permissible_limits_all_states_2023_2019.pdf
According to the recorded data, in Sriganganagar district of Rajasthan, 24% of the
samples analyzed had nitrate beyond the permissible limit (>45 mg/l) in 2019,
whereas in 2023 the proportion was 45%. Similarly, in Hanumangarh, the
percentage of samples reporting nitrate above permissible limits were 26% in 2019
and 56% in 2023 respectively.
1 3.02.2025 1019
(b) The Government is taking several measures to promote sustainable
agriculture in the country with a vision to discourage excessive use of chemical
fertilizers and promote organic agricultural practices. The Government is
implementing Soil Health Management and Soil Health Card Schemes under the
National Project on Soil Health and Fertility of National Mission for Sustainable
Agriculture since the year 2014-15. Soil health card provides information to farmers
on nutrient status of their soil along with recommendations on appropriate dosage
of nutrients to be applied for improving soil health and its productivity. Based on
the recommendations on Soil Health Card (SHC), so far, 93781 farmer’s trainings
and 7425 farmer’s melas/campaigns have been organized across the country for
promoting judicious use of chemical fertilizers including secondary and
micronutrients in conjunction with organic manures and bio-fertilizers.
Further, the Government is also promoting Natural Farming since 2019-2020
through BharatiyaPrakritikKrishi Paddhati (BPKP) programme under
Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY). The scheme mainly emphasizes on
exclusion of all synthetic chemical inputs and promotes on-farm biomass recycling
with major stress on biomass mulching, use of cow dung-urine formulations and
other plant based preparations.
(c) Water is a state subject and the responsibility of ground water management,
including taking initiatives for improving ground water quality and mitigate the
contamination issue, lies primarily with the state governments. However, several
steps have been taken by the Central Government in this direction like regular
quality monitoring and sharing of data by CGWB with state governments and other
1 3.02.2025 1020
stakeholders, taking up construction of Arsenic and Fluoride safe wells and
disseminating the technology, implementation of Water (Prevention and Control)
Act, 1974 and the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 by CPCB/SPCBs to prevent
and control pollution in water etc.
But the major thrust for safeguarding the entire population of the country from the
adverse effects of contaminated water has been provided by the Government by
way of implementation of Jal Jeevan Mission (JJM) – Har Ghar Jal, as a novel
initiative. JJM is operational in the country since August 2019, including in the
states of Rajasthan, Punjab and Karnataka, with a view to make provision of
potable tap water supply in adequate quantity, of prescribed quality and on regular
and long-term basis to every rural household in the country. Under JJM, Bureau of
Indian Standards’ BIS:10500 standards have been adopted as prescribed norms
for quality of tap water service delivery and JJM guidelines also stipulate that while
allocating the funds to States/ UTs, 10% weightage is to be given to the population
residing in habitations affected by chemical contaminants.
(d) Realizing the significance of having high frequency data on ground water on
real time basis, this Ministry has taken up the process of installing Digital Water
Level Recorders (DWLRs) with telemetry systems throughout the country under its
various schemes and projects like Ground Water Management and Regulation
(GWM andR) Scheme, Atal Bhujal Yojana etc. The state governments are also
funded for carrying out the said activity under National Hydrology Project(NHP). So
far, around 24,000 DWLRs have been installed across the country under the above
said schemes, which facilitate real-time access to ground water related data.
1 3.02.2025 1021
(e) As stated earlier, Water being a State subject, sustainable development and
management of groundwater resources is primarily the responsibility of the State
Governments. However, the Central Government facilitates the efforts of the State
Governments by way of technical and financial assistance through its various
schemes and projects. In this direction, the important steps taken by the Ministry
of Jal Shakti and other central ministries for sustainable development of ground
water resources in the country, with special focus on water stressed regions, are
given below:-
i.The Government is implementing Jal Shakti Abhiyan (JSA) in the country since
2019 which is a mission mode and time bound programme for harvesting the
rainfall and taking up water conservation activities. Currently, JSA 2024 is being
implemented in the country with special focus on 151 water stressed districts of the
country. JSA is an umbrella campaign under which various ground water recharge
and conservation related works are being taken up in convergence with various
central and state schemes.
ii.CGWB has taken up National Aquifer Mapping and Management Programme
(NAQUIM) with an aim to delineate aquifer disposition and their characterization.
Entire mappable area of the country of around 25 lakh sq. km has been mapped
under the scheme and management plans have been shared with the respective
State governments for implementation.
iii.Master Plan for Artificial Recharge to Groundwater- 2020 has been prepared by
the CGWB for the entire country and shared with States/UTs providing a broad
outline for construction of around 1.42 crore rain water harvesting and artificial
1 3.02.2025 1022
recharge structures in the country to harness around 185 BCM (Billion cubic meter)
of water.
iv.MoJS is implementing Atal Bhujal Yojana, which is a community led scheme for
participatory ground water management focusing on demand side management of
ground water in 80 water stressed districts in 7 States.
v.Department of Agriculture and Farmers’ Welfare (DA and FW), GoI, is
implementing Per Drop More Crop (PDMC) Scheme in the country since 2015-
16, which focuses on enhancing water use efficiency at farm level through Micro
Irrigation and better on-farm water management practices to optimize the use of
available water resources.
vi.Mission Amrit Sarovar was launched by the Government of India which aimed at
developing and rejuvenating at least 75 water bodies in each district of the country.
As an outcome nearly 69,000 Amrit Sarovars have been constructed/rejuvenated
in the country.
(f) Use of Ground Water for drinking purpose having Arsenic, Fluoride, heavy
metals etc. above the permissible limits over a prolonged period of time is known
to cause several adverse health effects. As per the information provided by M/o
Health and Family Welfare, Arsenic exposure can cause skin lesions, cancer,
cardiovascular diseases and developmental effects in children. Likewise,
excessive fluoride in the ground water can result in dental and skeletal Fluorosis.
Similarly, various other contaminants are known to produce different kinds of
adverse effects.
1 3.02.2025 1023
फतेहपुर म(cid:164) सीवर लाइन/जल शोधन संय(cid:253)ं
1595. (cid:174)ी. नरशे चं(cid:254) उ(cid:194)त म पटेल:

EXTENSION OF THE UDAN-3.0 SCHEME
1596. SHRI JAGADISH SHETTAR:
Will the Minister of CIVIL AVIATION be pleased to state whether the Government
is considering to extend the UDAN-3.0 Scheme for any further period and if so,
the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF COOPERATION; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CIVIL AVIATION (SHRI
MURLIDHAR MOHOL):
Under the Regional Connectivity Scheme-Ude Desh ka Aam Nagrik (RCS-
UDAN), routes are awarded for a period of three (03) years from the date of
commencement of RCS flight operations for each version of the Scheme including
UDAN 3.0.
The government has announced launching of a modified UDAN Scheme for the
next 10 years.
CESS LEVIED THROUGH PETROLEUM PRODUCTS
1597. SHRI RAO RAJENDRA SINGH:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any data on the amount of cess levied through
various petroleum products and if so, the details thereof; and
(b) whether the Government has any account of utilisation of the aforementioned
cess and if so, the details thereof along with the details of the petroleum
infrastructure devolved to States for the development?
1 3.02.2025 1025
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND NATURAL
GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM (SHRI
SURESH GOPI):
(a) The details of taxes imposed on Petrol, Diesel, LPG are as follows:
Petrol and Diesel:
Currently, an excise duty of Rs. 19.90 per litre and Rs. 15.80 per litre is levied on
petrol and diesel respectively. The Union Government levies Central Excise/Cess
on petrol/diesel, while the State Governments levy Value Added Tax (VAT). The
details of VAT levied by various State Governments on Petrol and Diesel are given
in the enclosed Statement-I.
LPG: The rates of Customs duty and GST applicable on LPG are:
Customs

domestic LPG.
Source: Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC)
The details of contribution of petroleum sector to exchequer since 2023-24 are
given in the enclosed Statement-II.
1 3.02.2025 1026
(b) The revenue generated by taxation is used in various infrastructural projects
and developmental schemes of the Government like Targeted subsidy to PMUY
households, Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), Pradhan Mantri
Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY), Ayushman Bharat, Pradhan Mantri Garib Kalyan Anna
Yojana (PMGKAY), free vaccination for COVID – 19 etc.

HOUSING SUPPORT TO THE HOMELESS POPULATION
1601. SHRI E. T. MOHAMMED BASHEER:
Will the Minister of HOUSING AND URBAN AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to reduce homelessness and
provide housing support to the homeless population;
(b) whether the Government has started any specific programmes/initiatives aimed
at providing housing for vulnerable populations, such as elderly, disabled or
veterans and if so, the details thereof; and
1 3.02.2025 1038
(c) the manner in which the Government is addressing the issues of eviction
prevention, especially in urban areas with high rent costs?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HOUSING AND URBAN
AFFAIRS (SHRI TOKHAN SAHU):
(a) to (c)‘Land’ and ‘Colonization’ are State subjects. Therefore, schemes related
to housing for their citizens including homeless and vulnerable populations, such
as elderly, disabled or veterans in their respective urban areas are implemented by
States/Union Territories (UTs). However, Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
(MoHUA) supplements the efforts of States/UTs by providing Central Assistance
under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U) since 25.06.2015 with an
aim to provide all weather pucca houses with basic civic amenities to eligible urban
beneficiaries including homeless population across the country.
Based on project proposals submitted by the State/UT Governments under
PMAY-U, a total of 118.64 lakh houses have been sanctioned by the Ministry, out
of which 112.46 lakh have been grounded and 90.36 lakh are completed/delivered
to the beneficiaries including homeless and vulnerable populations in urban areas
as on 03.02.2025. The remaining houses are at different stages of construction.
The scheme period has been extended up to 31.12.2025to complete all the houses
sanctioned without changing the funding pattern and implementation methodology.
Further, based on the learning from the experiences of 9 years
implementation of PMAY-U, MoHUA has revamped the scheme and launched
PMAY-U 2.0 ‘Housing for All’ Mission with effect from 01.09.2024 for
implementation in urban areas across the country to construct, purchase and rent
1 3.02.2025 1039
a house by 1 crore additional eligible beneficiaries at affordable cost through four
verticals i.e., Beneficiary Led Construction (BLC), Affordable Housing in
Partnership (AHP), Affordable Rental Housing (ARH) and Interest Subsidy Scheme
(ISS). As per the scheme guidelines of PMAY-U 2.0, preference under the scheme
is given to persons belonging to SC/ST, minorities, widows, single women, persons
with disabilities, senior citizens, transgenders and other weaker and vulnerable
sections of the society.
Additionally, the Government also supported Shelter for Urban Homeless
under Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana -National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-
NULM) to ensure availability and access of the urban homeless population to
permanent shelters including the basic infrastructure facilities like water supply,
sanitation, safety and security.
Tenancy is governed by State tenancy laws to balance interest of landlord
and tenant. This Ministry has circulated Model Tenancy Act to States for adoption
or adaptation to local scenario.
िबजली कटौती क(cid:236) बढ़ती सम(cid:214) या

क(cid:236) िव(cid:309)सनीयता और गणु व(cid:176)ा म (cid:164)सधु ार करने म (cid:164)मदद िमलेगी। रा(cid:186)यवार (cid:202)यौरा संल(cid:181)न िववरण-I म (cid:164)िदया
गया है।
इसके अलावा, भारत सरकार संशोिधत िवतरण (cid:177)े(cid:253) (cid:214)क(cid:236)म (आरडीएसएस) क(cid:236) (cid:214)क(cid:236)म के तहत सौभा(cid:181)य

करने के िलए िवतरण यूिटिलटी (cid:300)ारा सव(cid:165)(cid:177)ण िकया गया है। जहां भी संभव पाया गया, आरडीएसएस के
तहत ि(cid:250)ड आधा(cid:229)रत िव(cid:299)तु ीकरण काय(cid:170) सं(cid:214)वीकृत िकए गए ह(cid:167)। आज तक, 9,97,680 घर(cid:332) के ि(cid:250)ड

समाधान िकया जाना है।

जल शि(cid:265) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा रेल म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी वी. सोम(cid:198)ना):
(क) स े (ग) अग(cid:214)त 2019 स,े भारत सरकार रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) क(cid:236) भागीदारीसे जल जीवन िमशन (जेजेएम)-हर

(cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) रहने वाले (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) सिहत देश के (cid:255)(cid:194)येक (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार हेतुपीनेयो(cid:181)य पानी का (cid:255)ावधान
िकया जा सके ।
अग(cid:214)त 2019 म(cid:164) जल जीवन िमशन क(cid:236) शु(cid:321)आत म(cid:164), के वल 3.23 करोड़ (16.8%) (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) के
पास नल जल कने(cid:179)शन होने क(cid:236) सूचना थी। अब तक, रा(cid:186)य(cid:332)/संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)(cid:253)े (cid:332) (cid:300)ारासूिचत िकए गए
अनसु ार, 10.02.2025 तक, जल जीवन िमशन (जेजएे म) – हर घर जल के तहत लगभग 12.22 करोड़
और (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) को नल जल कने(cid:179)शन (cid:255)दान िकए गए ह(cid:167)। इस (cid:255)कार, 10.02.2025 तक, देश के
19.36 करोड़ स े अिधक (cid:250)ामीण प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) म(cid:164) स े 15.45 करोड़ (79.79%) स े अिधक प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) के पास
उनके घर(cid:332) म(cid:164) नल जल आपूित(cid:170) होने क(cid:236) सूचना है।
जल जीवन िमशन के तहत िनिध का आवंटन करते समय नल जल कने(cid:179)शन क(cid:236) कवरेज को (cid:255)ाथिमकता
देने के िलए दगु (cid:170)मभू-भाग(cid:332)को 30% भारांक मह(cid:194)व िदया जाता ह ै िजसम(cid:164)अ(cid:198)यबात(cid:332)के साथ-
साथम(cid:322)भूिमिवकासकाय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (डीडीपी) और सूखा(cid:255)वण (cid:177)े(cid:253) काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (डीपीएपी) के अंतग(cid:170)त आने वाल े
(cid:177)े(cid:253) शािमल ह(cid:167)।
इसके अलावा, पूर े देश म(cid:164) जेजेएम क(cid:236) योजना बनान े और उसेतेजी स े लागू करन े के िलए, संय(cid:265)ु िवचार-

को अंितम (cid:322)प देने, काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन क(cid:236) िनयिमत समी(cid:177)ा, (cid:177)मता िनमा(cid:170)ण, (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण, (cid:178)ानसाझा करन े के िलए
काय(cid:170)शालाओ/ंस(cid:204)मेलन(cid:332)/वेिबनार, तकनीक(cid:236) सहायता (cid:255)दान करन ेके िलए बह(cid:242)-िवषयकटीम (cid:300)ारा (cid:177)े(cid:253) दौर,े

(cid:255)बंधन काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (आईड(cid:202)(cid:208)यूएमपी), रा(cid:186)य योजनाओ,ं एमपी/एमएलए-एलएडीिनिध,

इसके अलावा, जल शि(cid:265)अिभयान: कै च द रेन (जेएसए: सीटीआर) अिभयान 2019 म(cid:164) श(cid:322)ु िकया गया
था। इसका उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य देश के 256 जल संकट वाल े िजल(cid:332) म(cid:164) लोग(cid:332) क(cid:236) भागीदारी सेजमीनी (cid:214)तर पर जल
संर(cid:177)ण को (cid:255)ो(cid:194)सािहतकरना है। इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265), िवशेष (cid:322)प स े पेयजल उपल(cid:202)धता के िलए (cid:214)थायी जल
(cid:255)बंधन के मह(cid:194)व को (cid:214)वीकार करते ह(cid:242)ए, जेएसए-सीटीआर को 2023 म(cid:164) "पेयजल के िलए (cid:261)ोत ि(cid:214)थरता"
िवषय के साथलागू िकया गया था। इसी तरह, 2024 म(cid:164), जेएसए को "नारीशि(cid:265) स े जल शि(cid:265)" िवषय के
साथलागू िकया गया था, िजसम(cid:164) जल संर(cid:177)ण के (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) मिहलाओ ं(cid:300)ारा िनभाई गई मह(cid:194)वपूण(cid:170)भूिमका पर
जोर िदया गया था।

1605. (cid:174)ी िदल(cid:212)े वर कामैत:
(cid:179)या सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:

प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंक(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या िकतनी है;

(ग) उ(cid:265) सड़क का िनमा(cid:170)ण कब तक पूरा होने क(cid:236) संभावना है; और

(cid:322)प स े (cid:250)ामीण और सदु रु (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म (cid:164) रहन े वाले मिहलाओ ं और प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) के जीवन पर मह(cid:194)वपणू (cid:170) (cid:255)भाव
डाला है। कुछ (cid:255)मुख लाभ(cid:332) का सं(cid:177)ेप म(cid:164) नीचे उ(cid:208) लेख िकया गया है
(i) पीएमयूवाई के प(cid:229)रणाम(cid:214)व(cid:322)प पारंप(cid:229)रक खाना पकाने के तरीक(cid:332) म(cid:164) बदलाव आया है िजसम(cid:164) लकड़ी,
गोबर और फसल अवशेष(cid:332) जैसे ठोस पदाथ(cid:334) को जलाना शािमल है। (cid:214)व(cid:184)छ ईधं न के उपयोग से घर के
अंदर वाय ु (cid:255)दूषण म(cid:164) कमी आई है। इससे (cid:309)सन (cid:214)वा(cid:214)(cid:195)य म(cid:164) सधु ार ह(cid:242)आ ह,ै खासकर मिहलाओ ं और
ब(cid:184)च(cid:332) म(cid:164) जो पारंप(cid:229)रक (cid:322)प से घरले ू धुए ं के संपक(cid:170) म(cid:164) अिधक आते ह(cid:167)।

(ii) (cid:250)ामीण (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) रहने वाले प(cid:229)रवार, खास तौर पर दूरदराज के इलाक(cid:332) म(cid:164) रहने वाले प(cid:229)रवार, अ(cid:179)सर
अपने समय और ऊजा(cid:170) का एक बड़ा िह(cid:214)सा पारंप(cid:229)रक खाना पकाने के ईधं न को इक(cid:281)ा करने म(cid:164) खच(cid:170)
करते ह(cid:167)। एलपीजी ने गरीब प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) क(cid:236) मिहलाओ ंके िलए खाना पकाने म(cid:164) लगने वाले समय और मेहनत
को कम िकया है। इस (cid:255)कार, उनके पास उपल(cid:202)ध खाली समय का उपयोग आिथ(cid:170)क उ(cid:194)पादकता बढ़ाने
के िलए कई (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) िकया जा सकता है।
(iii) बायोमास और पारपं (cid:229)रक ईधं न से एलपीजी पर प(cid:229)रवत(cid:170)न करने से खाना पकाने के िलए लकड़ी और
अ(cid:198)य बायोमास पर िनभ(cid:170)रता कम हो जाती है, िजससे वन(cid:332) क(cid:236) कटाई और पया(cid:170)वरण (cid:177)रण म(cid:164) कमी आती
है। इससे न के वल प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) को लाभ होता है बि(cid:208)क (cid:211)यापक पया(cid:170)वरण सरं (cid:177)ण (cid:255)यास(cid:332) म (cid:164) भी योगदान
िमलता है
(iv) बेहतर खाना पकाने क(cid:236) सिु वधाओ ं के साथ, पोषण पर संभािवत सकारा(cid:194)मक (cid:255)भाव पड़ता है।
प(cid:229)रवार(cid:332) को िविभ(cid:198)न (cid:255)कार के पौि(cid:310)क भोजन पकाना आसान लग सकता है, िजससे सम(cid:250) (cid:214)वा(cid:214)(cid:195)य
बेहतर होता है।

1607. (cid:174)ी आशीष दुब:े

(क) (cid:179)या देश म(cid:164) कृिष (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण (cid:179)ल(cid:214)टर योजना से बड़ी सं(cid:180)या म(cid:164) लोग लाभाि(cid:198)वत हो रहे ह(cid:167);
(ख) यिद हां, तो िवगत पांच वष(cid:334) का त(cid:194)संबंधी रा(cid:186)यवार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है; और
(ग) यिद नह(cid:233), तो इस योजना के (cid:255)भावी काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन के िलए सरकार (cid:300)ारा (cid:179)या िदशािनद(cid:165)श जारी

(क) और (ख) खा(cid:299) (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण उ(cid:299)ोग म(cid:253)ं ालय (एमओएफपीआई) (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी िकसान संपदा योजना
(पीएमके एसवाई) के अंतग(cid:170)त एक उप-योजना, कृिष (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण (cid:179)ल(cid:214)टर (एपीसी) हेतु अवसंरचना िनमा(cid:170)ण
क(cid:236) योजना को काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रहा है। एपीसी योजना के उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य इस (cid:255)कार ह(cid:167):

उ(cid:194)पादन (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के िनकट खा(cid:299) (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण के िलए आधुिनक अवसंरचनाका िनमा(cid:170)ण करना।

खेत से लेकर उपभो(cid:265)ा तक एक(cid:236)कृत एवं स(cid:204)पूण(cid:170) प(cid:229)रर(cid:177)ण अवसरं चना सिु वधाए ंउपल(cid:202)ध कराना।

अ(cid:184)छी तरह से सुसि(cid:186)जत आपूित(cid:170) (cid:174)ंखृ ला के मा(cid:197)यम से उ(cid:194)पादक(cid:332)/िकसान(cid:332) के समूह(cid:332) को खा(cid:299)
(cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करणकता(cid:170)ओ ंऔर बाजार(cid:332) से जोड़कर (cid:255)भावी बैकवड(cid:170) एडं फॉरवड(cid:170) िलंके ज का िनमा(cid:170)ण करना
एपीसी योजना के अंतग(cid:170)त योजना क(cid:236) शु(cid:321)आत से अब तक 75 कृिष (cid:255)स(cid:214)ं करण (cid:179)ल(cid:214)टर प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ं
को मंजूरी दी गई है। इन प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंस े 77011 लोग(cid:332) के िलए रोजगार सिृ जत ह(cid:242)आ ह/ै सिृ जत होने क(cid:236)
संभावना है तथा 289775 िकसान लाभाि(cid:198)वत हो रहे ह(cid:167)/लाभाि(cid:198)वत होने वाले ह(cid:167)।
(cid:255)ारभं से अब तक अनुमोिदत एपीसी प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंका रा(cid:186)यवार (cid:202)यौरा संल(cid:181)न िववरण म (cid:164) िदया गया
है ।

SAFETY MEASURES IN PETROLEUM SECTOR
1608. SHRI SUNIL DATTATREY TATKARE:
Will the Minister of PETROLEUM AND NATURAL GAS be pleased to state:
(a) whether any measures have been taken/proposed to be taken by the
Government for providing safety from the petroleum products being hazardous and
inflammable in nature including the installation of advanced lightning protection
system by the oil industry to prevent theoccurrence of fire incidents at par with the
worldwide practices being followed to deal with suchsituations; and
(b) if so, the details thereof?
1 3.02.2025 1078
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF PETROLEUM AND NATURAL
GAS; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF TOURISM (SHRI
SURESH GOPI):
(a) and (b) Oil and Gas companies in the country are required to adhere to all
relevant standards of safety. Oil Industry Safety Directorate (OISD), a technical
directorate under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, has been entrusted
with the responsibility of formulating standards, overseeing its implementation
through safety audits in petroleum industry with the aim to enhancing safety levels
and reducing risks inherent with this industry. The industry conducts regular risk
assessments and hazard analyses to identify vulnerable areas and apply
appropriate protective measures. The safety measures required to be followed by
oil andgas companies inter alia includes routine inspections, testing, and
maintenance of the lightning protection systems to ensure their optimal
functionality. OISD conducts external safety audits of oil and gas installations at
defined intervals, verifying compliance with the requirements outlined in the OISD
Standards.

एक(cid:236)कृत िकया, िजससे उनक(cid:236) उपल(cid:202) धता और रखरखाव सुिनि(cid:306)त ह(cid:242)आ।

कना(cid:170)टक और तिमलनाडु म(cid:164), सीएससी और आईएचएचएल के स(cid:335)दय(cid:234)करण ने (cid:214)व(cid:184)छता
अवसंरचना को सामदु ाियक (cid:322)प से (cid:255)िस(cid:294) (cid:214)थल(cid:332) म(cid:164) बदल िदया।
इस अिभयान म(cid:164) सामूिहक कार(cid:170)वाई के मह(cid:194)व को दशा(cid:170)ते ह(cid:242)ए रा(cid:186)यपाल(cid:332), क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय मंि(cid:253)य(cid:332), सांसद(cid:332), रा(cid:186)य
मंि(cid:253)य(cid:332), िवधायक(cid:332) और पंचायत नेताओ ंक(cid:236) सि(cid:248)य भागीदारी दखे ी गई। रा(cid:186)य, िजला और (cid:250)ाम पंचायत
(cid:214)तर(cid:332) पर जल और (cid:214)व(cid:184)छता सिमितय(cid:332) को सि(cid:248)य िकया गया, िजससे दीघ(cid:170)कािलक जवाबदेही और

तक पह(cid:242)ंच िवशेष (cid:322)प से मिहलाओ ंऔर सीमांत समदु ाय(cid:332) के िलए ग(cid:229)रमा, सरु (cid:177)ा तथा समानता सुिनि(cid:306)त

(ख) से (घ) (cid:214)व(cid:184)छता रा(cid:186)य का िवषय है। एसबीएमजी चरण-II काय(cid:170)संबंधी िदशािनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) के अनुसार,

PROF. SOUGATA RAY (DUM DUM): Sir, I beg to oppose the introduction of the
Income-Tax Bill, 2025. … (Interruptions) The Income-tax Act, 1961 has been
1 3.02.2025 1121
serving its purpose while it was amended every year. … (Interruptions) The
amendments were the result of the Government’s decisions. In a new Bill, they will
only have to suffer the same fate as the Government introduced the amendments
in new words… (Interruptions) No new Bill needs to be introduced because the
present one has a complex language. … (Interruptions) The logic is that because
of complex language there will be increasing percentage of compliance cannot
hold. … (Interruptions) The basic structure of the income tax remains the same
because of its very nature, and no purpose would be served by introducing a new
Income-Tax Bill to help tax administration… (Interruptions)
Sir, the new law has 622 pages, 526 Sections, 26 Chapters and 16
Schedules. … (Interruptions) Is this a simple law? … (Interruptions) The old law
had 298 Sections and 135 Schedules. … (Interruptions) So, there is no
simplification. I oppose the introduction of the Bill.
14.12 hrs
At this stage S/Shri Kalyan Banerjee, Dharmendra Yadav, Imran Masood
and some other hon. Members came and stood on the floor near the Table.
SHRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN: Sir, with your permission, three Members
have mentioned about their objections to the introduction of this Bill. I would like to
say that actually Shri N.K. Premachandran’s objection does not seem to be correct
at all, and that is also mentioned in my introductory remarks. The Income-Tax Act
was originally enacted in 1961, and it came into effect in 1962. At that time, it had
only 298 sections. That is correctly referred. But then, as time went by, year after
year, many more sections were added. As it stands today, there are 819 sections.
1 3.02.2025 1122
From those 819 sections, we are bringing it down to 536 sections. So, he should
look at what it is today rather than what it was in 1961. So, on that objection, I do
not think he is right. He should understand where the law is today and where it has
been reduced.
Secondly, Shri Manish Tewari’s argument was this. Sections are more now
rather than when it was in 1961. That is a point. That I have answered. Then,
4000 amendments have been made from 1961. All those are being looked at now
and being reduced now. So, Shri Manish Tewari’s core argument that it contains
536 clauses and has also more number of amendments now, I think that he has
not even looked into the papers, I am sorry to say. So, it cannot be sustained. Shri
N.K. Premachandran’s statement about objects and reasons being different is also
not correct. I can explain in detail the objects and reasons when we will take it up
for discussion. But it cannot be sustained as an objection.
Thirdly, coming to Prof. Sougata Ray’s comment regarding the philosophy
and also the number of words, and so on, he is thinking that it is only a mere
mechanical change. It is not a mechanical change. Substantial changes are being
made. The number of words has come down by half. Where there were five-and-
a-half lakh words, now it has come down by half. Sections have come down.
Chapters have come down. It is in plain simple English and in plain simple Hindi.
… (Interruptions) Therefore, these are not sustainable.

उसका संशोधन करने वाले िवधेयक को परु : (cid:214)थािपत करने क(cid:236) अनुमित (cid:255)दान क(cid:236)

जाए ।”

(vii) Need for official recognition for celebration of Sant Shri Sevalal
Maharaj Jayanthi on 15th February
SHRIMATI D. K. ARUNA (MAHBUBNAGAR): I would like to bring to the kind
notice of the Government the need to declare and give official recognition and
nationwide celebrations of Sant Shri Sevalal Maharaj Jayanthi on 15th February
every year. In this regard, I would like to state that 12 crore Banjaras in the country
have significantly contributed to the nation's welfare, especially during times when
1 3.02.2025 1131
there were no roads and they pioneered transportation creating trade routes to
carry essential goods and also helped Army during battles. Unfortunately, despite
their sacrifices and contributions, the rich history of the Banjaras remain
unrecognized in written records. At a time of socio-economic despair with Banjaras,
Sant Shri Sevalal Maharaj emerged as a guiding light, blessed by Goddess
Jagadamba who is callipered and a reformer, he dedicated his life as a social
reformer, spiritual leader, pioneer, warrior and mentor to lead the Banjaras into a
righteous path and today he is worshipped as a spiritual guru in the form of God
with grand Bhog, Bhandaras conducted worldwide in his honour. States like
Telangana, AP, Karnataka and Maharashtra have already officially recognized and
celebrated his Jayanthi on 15th February every year and by recognizing his
services at the national level, this would be an acknowledgement of his
unparalleled social and humanitarian contributions in recognition of his invaluable
services to the nation. Therefore, considering the deep sentiments of Banjaras
national and worldwide, I humbly request the Hon'ble Minister of Social Justice and
Empowerment to kindly declare and give official recognition and nationwide
celebrations of Shri Sevalal Maharaj Jayanthi on 15th February every year.

(ix) Need for urgent repairing and maintenance of breakwater at Androth
Island in Lakshadweep
SHRI HAMDULLAH SAYEED (LAKSHADWEEP): I would like to draw the
attention of the Government to the urgent need for maintenance and repair of the
breakwater at Androth Island, which is crucial for ensuring the safety and
functionality of the island’s maritime infrastructure. Several essential works,
including the de-silting of the main wharf area, the retrieval and placement of
tetrapods, and the supply of granite stone boulders for backfilling, are pending for
approval from the authorities. Despite estimates being submitted, the delay in
approval and execution of these works is putting the infrastructure at risk. The
tender process for casting 8T tetrapods is in progress, while the casting and
placement of 8T tetrapods at the seaside of the breakwater await approval. These
structures are critical for mitigating the impact of strong Hitting waves and
protecting the breakwater. The maintenance of the IIF Wharf passenger shutter
remains unfinished, affecting operations and connectivity. A major obstacle is the
lack of a suitable crane. The retrieval and placement of 8T/12T tetrapods, awarded
in 2023, have not begun due to the absence of a 100-ton capacity crane. The
1 3.02.2025 1133
existing 30T crane is insufficient. I urge the Government to approve pending
estimates, procure a high-capacity crane, and expedite the necessary work.
(x) Need to expedite implementation of Jal Jeevan Mission in Kerala
SHRI BENNY BEHANAN (CHALAKUDY): I wish to draw the attention of the
Government to the serious delays in the implementation of the Jal Jeevan Mission
(JJM) in Kerala. The Mission, which aims to provide Functional Household Tap
Connections (FHTCs) to all rural households, is facing hurdles due to fund
shortages and non-disbursal of payments to contractors. This has significantly
slowed down progress, affecting thousands of households. Moreover, roads dug
up for JJM pipeline works remain unrestored, causing severe inconvenience to the
public. Many panchayat roads are in a dilapidated state, posing safety hazards to
commuters and residents. The delay in releasing payments has further stalled road
restoration efforts. I urge the Government to immediately address the issue by
releasing pending funds, ensuring timely payments to contractors, and directing
authorities to restore affected roads at the earliest. The success of JJM depends
on efficient execution, and any delay undermines the purpose of providing safe
drinking water. I seek the intervention of the Hon’ble Minister to take urgent action
in this regard.
(xi) Need to fill up the vacancies in National Commission for Backward
Classes (NCBC) and ensure its effective functionality
SHRI B. MANICKAM TAGORE (VIRUDHUNAGAR):The prolonged vacancies in
the National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC), a constitutional body that
plays a vital role in safeguarding the rights of our backward classes. For over a
1 3.02.2025 1134
year, the positions of Vice-Chairperson and two members of the NCBC have
remained vacant, severely affecting its functioning. This is not just a procedural
lapse; it is a direct violation of the promise made to uplift and protect the interests
of the backward classes in our country. The NCBC's mandate includes making
recommendations for the inclusion of communities in the list of backward classes,
investigating and monitoring complaints of discrimination, and advising the
Government on matters related to social justice. The absence of key members in
this Commission has paralyzed its work, leaving the backward classes without a
proper forum to address their grievances and seek justice. This is a matter of deep
concern, as it reflects the government's indifference toward the welfare of the most
marginalized sections of society. I urge the Government to immediately fill these
vacancies and ensure that the NCBC can function effectively, as it is crucial for the
social and economic advancement of backward classes. The time to act is now.
(xii) Regarding adverse impact on steel industries due to rise in prices of
PNG in Mandi Gobindgarh, Punjab
DR. AMAR SINGH (FATEHGARH SAHIB): Mandi Gobindgarh is the Steel industry
hub of north India. It is part of Shri Fatehgarh Sahib Constituency. Once a bustling
centre of iron and steel industry it has been affected in the recent past due to faulty
policies and unfavorable trade conditions. Majority of the units are MSMEs, yet they
collectively give employment to thousands of people. At present, the owners of
these Industries are protesting that PNG pipelines were laid and owners of re-
rolling units had spent crores on installing gas machinery and over a period of time,
companies started increasing the price of PNG, forcing the steel industries in
1 3.02.2025 1135
Punjab to again use coal or to shut down, so as to compete with the states where
fossil fuel was still being used. Therefore, Government should consider and revise
the pricing so that the industry and environment both should not be impacted. I
request the Government to depute senior officers to meet with the industry groups
from Mandi Gobindgarh and offer the maximum policy relief possible.
(xiii) Need to restore allocation of funds under various scholarship schemes
meant for students belonging to marginalized communities
DR. SHASHI THAROOR (THIRUVANANTHAPURAM): I wish to draw the Union
Government’s attention to the proposed drastic budget cuts to key scholarship
programmes, which would severely impact marginalised students' access to higher
education. The Union Budget 2025 proposes a 99.8% cut to the National Overseas
Scholarship, reducing it from Rs. 6 crore in revised estimates (RE) 2024 to Rs. 0.01
crore in budget estimates (BE) 2025. Similarly, the National Fellowship and
Scholarship for Higher Education of ST Students proposes a 99.99% reduction,
from Rs. 240 crore in RE 2024 to Rs. 0.02 crore in BE 2025. Additionally, the Pre-
Matric and Post-Matric Scholarships for Minorities are set to decrease by 72.4%
and 69.9%, respectively according to the proposed Budget. Additionally, the
Parliamentary Standing Committee on Social Justice and Empowerment has
pointed out that no new schemes have been introduced to replace the Maulana
Azad National Fellowship and the Padho Pardesh Interest Subsidy Scheme which
were discontinued in 2022-23 due to overlaps with other government programs. I,
therefore, urge the Ministers of Finance and other concerned Ministries to take
immediate measures to restore funding for these crucial scholarships and ensure
1 3.02.2025 1136
that financial constraints do not prevent students from disadvantaged backgrounds

1 3.02.2025 1137
(xvi) Need to recognize Gangasagar Mela observed in Sagar Island, West
Bengal as a National Mela
SHRIMATI JUNE MALIAH (MEDINIPUR): According to mythological,
archaeological and historical evidences, Gangasagar Mela is the largest and oldest
annual religious congregation of our country. It has been held at Sagar Island
continuously for the last three millennia. It is a place on Sunderban delta where
river Ganga meets the sea and a small temple with holy sage Kapil Muni, sage
Bhagirath and the Ganga exists. The legend is associated with the story of King
Bhagirath who performed penance and brought the sacred river Ganga from
heaven to earth to free the souls of his ancestors, 60,000 sons of King Sagar, who
were cursed by Maharishi Kapil Muni and turned to ashes. River-goddess Ganga
descended here making Gangasagar a revered place to worship, perform rituals
and seek spiritual solace. It is said that on Makar Sakranti, the Ganga reached the
sage's 'Ashram' (hermitage) at Sagardweep and the condemned were liberated.
Every year, in mid-January, on the occasion of Makar Sankranti, millions of Hindu
devotees from all over India and abroad assemble at the Sagar Island. It is believed
that a holy dip in the confluence of the Ganga and the Bay of Bengal will lead to
atonement for earthly sins and the achievement of Moksha or salvation. Thus, the
event has deep-rooted mythological and astrological significance, with this Mela
itself together with Kapil Muni and the Sagar Island having been mentioned in the
ancient Indian epics such the Ramayana, the Mahabharata and Kalidasa
Raghuvamsham. As it is totally isolated from the mainland, all pilgrims are bound
to cross the river Muriganga to reach the island, making it the only pilgrimage in
1 3.02.2025 1138
India, which is accessible solely through waterways. There is urgent need for a
bridge over Muriganga, for which detailed project report (DPR) has been completed
by state Government at an estimated cost of Rs. 1500 crore. Intangible Cultural
Heritage of India National Inventory can refer to "Social practices, rituals and festive
events", making Gangasagar Mela eligible to be included in the list of National
Melas like the festivals such as Deepavali, Chhat Puja and Kumbh Mela. Hence, I
am seeking attention of Government of India to recognize Gangasagar Mela as a
national Mela/a fair of national importance and placement of fund for this purpose.
(xvii) Need to take comprehensive measures to address the losses on
account of transmission and distribution of electricity in the country
DR. T. SUMATHY ALIAS THAMIZHACHI THANGAPANDIAN (CHENNAI
SOUTH): Transmission & Distribution (T&D) losses in the country is the major
reason for the shortage of power in the country. According to the Government
report, India's T&D losses have been over 20 percent of generation, which is more
than twice the world average. If only the government reduces the T&D losses the
power supply will see the brighter side. Tamil Nadu is one of the top industrial states
in the country and its power supply and demand alongwith the per capita
consumption too is on higher side. According to the Load Generation Balance
Report(LGBR) of the Southern Regional Power Committee(SRPC), TN's peak
demand in April is expected to be 20600MW, against the availability of 21,488MW,
resulting in a surplus of 985MW. But, in the evening, the requirement would be
20,700MW as against the availability of 16,003 MW, and leading to a deficit of
4,697MW. Steps are being taken by the Government of Tamil Nadu to ensure
1 3.02.2025 1139
uninterrupted supply. Ahead of summer, the TNEB and TANGEDCO officials have
identified overloading issues in 6,536 transformers across the state, of which, 407
have already been fixed. 48 new sub-stations have been installed to strengthen the
power grid along with the proposal to commission 20 power substations throughout
Tamil Nadu to provide an uninterrupted power supply. TANGEDCO is getting loans
from REC Limited and Power Finance Corporation Ltd., to enhance power
infrastructure at a rate of interest about 12.5%. The Government of India has been
requested for reducing the interest to 9%. Therefore, I urge the Union Government
to take all necessary steps to reduce the T & D losses in the country to less than
10%. Also I request the Union Government to provide adequate financial support
to develop state of art power infrastructure in Tamil Nadu with a reduced rate of
interest.
(xviii) Need to set up a Post Office Passport Seva Kendra in Periyar Nagar
area of North Chennai Parliamentary Constituency, Tamil Nadu
DR. KALANIDHI VEERASWAMY (CHENNAI NORTH): North Chennai, which
includes densely populated areas such as Periyar Nagar, has seen significant
growth in both residential and commercial sectors in recent years. Despite this, the
residents of these areas are forced to travel long distances to the nearest passport
office or regional passport office, which is inconvenient, costly, and time-
consuming. The establishment of a Post Office Passport Seva Kendra (POPSK) in
Periyar Nagar will significantly reduce the burden on local residents, especially in
terms of transportation and time spent traveling to distant locations. Currently, the
nearest POPSK is located far from the Periyar Nagar area. With the rapid
1 3.02.2025 1140
urbanization and increasing number of passport applications, it is imperative that a
passport service center is set up in this area. It is not uncommon for residents of
North Chennai to face long waiting periods or have to travel long distances to reach
an appointment at the nearest passport center. Being a representative of North
Chennai Parliamentary Constituency, I have requested vide my letters dated
10.08.2024, 08.09.2024 & 19.01.2025 but no action has been taken so far.
Therefore, I urge the Union Minister to open a Post Office Passport Seva Kendra
in Periyar Nagar area of North Chennai Constituency Parliamentary Constituency
as soon as possible.
(xix) Need to relocate the Jonnada toll gate at the bypass road near
Modavalasa-Chelluru in Andhra Pradesh
SHRI APPALANAIDU KALISETTI (VIZIANAGARAM): I would like to draw the
attention the Hon’ble Minister of Road Transport and Highways to a pressing issue
concerning the Vizag-Raipur Highway (NH-43). This vital highway, along with the
four-lane bypass road from Modavalasa to Gotlam on the outskirts of
Vizianagaram, was envisioned to enhance regional connectivity, boost economic
activity, and streamline freight movement across North Andhra and beyond.
However, the placement of a toll gate at Jonnada, located between Vizianagaram
and Visakhapatnam cities, undermines these objectives. This toll gate, instead of
being located on the bypass or the Vizag-Raipur Highway, has been placed on a
route heavily used by local commuters traveling between Vizianagaram and
Visakhapatnam. Thousands of daily commuters, who neither use the bypass nor
benefit directly from the highway infrastructure, are now being unfairly burdened
1 3.02.2025 1141
with toll fees. I urge the Hon’ble Minister to revisit the toll placement policies and
initiate a review of this matter. Corrective measures such as relocating the Jonnada
tollgate to the bypass road near Modavalasa-Chelluru, where it rightfully belongs,
would ensure fairness, equity, and public convenience. Let us reaffirm our
commitment to creating infrastructure that serves the people efficiently and justly.

(xxiii) Need to provide Rajasthan its share of water
from Ravi and Beas Rivers
(cid:174)ी हनुमान बने ीवाल (नागौर) : म (cid:167) माननीय जल शि(cid:265) म(cid:253)ं ी जी को रावी- (cid:211)यास नदी के जल म(cid:164) पंजाब

चाहता ह(cid:243)ं िक िदनांक 31.12.1981 को पंजाब, ह(cid:229)रयाणा व राज(cid:214)थान के म(cid:180)ु यमंि(cid:253)य(cid:332) के म(cid:197)य रावी-
(cid:211)यास निदय(cid:332) के आिध(cid:179)य जल के बंटवारे के बारे म(cid:164) एक समझौता ह(cid:242)आ िजसके अनुसार रावी-(cid:211)यास
निदय(cid:332) के आिध(cid:179)य जल म(cid:164) से राज(cid:214)थान के िलए भी उसके िह(cid:214)से का जल िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत िकया गया व
समझौते के कुछ वष(cid:333) बाद राज(cid:214)थान अपने िह(cid:214)से का पूण(cid:170) जल उपयोग करने क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित म(cid:164) हो गया और
राज(cid:214)थान सरकार (cid:300)ारा भाखड़ा (cid:211)यास (cid:255)बंधन मंडल, भारत सरकार तथा पंजाब सरकार को कई बार
(cid:255)ितवेदन भी िदए और पजं ाब सरकार जब राज(cid:214)थान को उसके िह(cid:214)से का पानी नह(cid:233) देने के उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य से
पंजाब समझौता समाि(cid:302) अिधिनयम 2014 लेकर आई तब भारत सरकार (cid:300)ारा भेजे गए इस िवषय का
परी(cid:177)ण करके माननीय सव(cid:333)(cid:184)चय (cid:198)यायालय न(cid:164) पंजाब सरकार (cid:300)ारा लाये गए अिधिनयम को संवैधािनक
(cid:255)ावधान(cid:332) के अनसु ार सही नह(cid:233) बताया और कहा िक 1981 के समझौते को पंजाब िनर(cid:214)त नह(cid:233) कर
सकता इसिलए क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार राज(cid:214)थान को उसके िह(cid:214)से का शेष जल शी(cid:325)् िदलवाने हेतु ह(cid:214)त(cid:177)ेप
कर (cid:164) ।
_________
14.18 hrs
OBSERVATION BY THE SPEAKER
Conclusion of the First Part of Budget Session
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) : माननीय सद(cid:214)यगण, आज बजट स(cid:253) के (cid:255)थम चरण का आिखरी िदन ह ै । मुझे खुशी
है िक इस स(cid:253) के दौरान हमने अ(cid:184)छे वातावरण म (cid:164) चचा(cid:170) क(cid:236) है । मुझे आपका पूरा सहयोग िमला ह ै ।
महामिहम रा(cid:213)(cid:367)पित जी के अिभभाषण पर ध(cid:198)यवाद (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव पर 17 घंटे और 23 िमनट क(cid:236) साथ(cid:170)क चचा(cid:170)
1 3.02.2025 1144
ह(cid:242)ई ह ै । इसम(cid:164) 173 माननीय सद(cid:214)य(cid:332) ने देर राि(cid:253) तक बैठकर अपने दािय(cid:194)व(cid:332) का िनव(cid:170)हन िकया है ।
बजट पर सामा(cid:198)य चचा(cid:170) 16 घंटे 13 िमनट तक चली, िजसम(cid:164) 170 सद(cid:214)य(cid:332) ने सि(cid:248)य भागीदारी
िनभाई । आप सबके सहयोग से इस स(cid:253) म(cid:164) हमारी (cid:255)ोडि(cid:179)टिवटी लगभग 112 (cid:255)ितशत रही ह ै। मुझे आशा
है िक आगे भी आपका इसी तरीके से सहयोग िमलता रहेगा ।
सभा क(cid:236) काय(cid:170)वाही सोमवार, िदनांक 10 माच(cid:170), 2025 को (cid:255)ात: 11 बज े तक के िलए (cid:214)थिगत क(cid:236)
जाती है ।
14.19 hrs
The Lok Sabha then adjourned till Eleven of the Clock
on Monday, March 10, 2025/ Phalguna 19, 1946 (Saka)
